blocks.html_en: The French government already did this to Pavel Durov: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arrest_and_indictment_of_Pavel... , which I'm sure people like Zuckerberg and Musk paid attention to and developed countermeasures for. After the arrest, Telegram started cooperating more with warrants from various countries' national police. Signal has better security and privacy guarantees than Telegram and is probably what ordinary people should be using instead of Telegram.

blocks.html_en:
> I think this is because the demand is so extreme for datacenters now that companies are building them in a manner that disrupts the environment around them.

This would make sense if the ones being targeted are the ones that do this, but that isn't the case. Moreover, those would be simple to address, e.g. pass an ordinance that prohibits long-term operation of fossil fuel generators, defined as operating them for more than 40 days out of each 700. Or impose a reasonable decibel limit as measured at the property line.

Meanwhile people are proposing "data center moratoriums" and so on, even if the data center would be built in a non-residential area with a closed-loop cooling system and generate its power from a completely silent and non-polluting solar+battery system. Which makes it pretty obvious that the motivation is something else.

sentences.en: > I think this is because the demand is so extreme for datacenters now that companies are building them in a manner that disrupts the environment around them.This would make sense if the ones being targeted are the ones that do this, but that isn't the case.
sentences.zh: > 我认为这是因为现在对数据中心的需求是如此极端，以至于公司正在以破坏周围环境的方式构建数据中心。如果目标是这样做的公司，那么这是有道理的，但事实并非如此。

sentences.en: Moreover, those would be simple to address, e.g. pass an ordinance that prohibits long-term operation of fossil fuel generators, defined as operating them for more than 40 days out of each 700.
sentences.zh: 此外，这些问题很容易解决，例如通过一项禁止长期运行化石燃料发电机的法令，即每 700 天运行时间超过 40 天。

sentences.en: Or impose a reasonable decibel limit as measured at the property line.Meanwhile people are proposing "data center moratoriums" and so on, even if the data center would be built in a non-residential area with a closed-loop cooling system and generate its power from a completely silent and non-polluting solar+battery system.
sentences.zh: 或者在物业线处测量合理的分贝限制。与此同时，人们提出“暂停数据中心”等建议，即使数据中心将建在非住宅区，配备闭环冷却系统，并通过完全静音、无污染的太阳能+电池系统发电。

blocks.html_en:
> I think this is because the demand is so extreme for datacenters now that companies are building them in a manner that disrupts the environment around them.

This would make sense if the ones being targeted are the ones that do this, but that isn't the case. Moreover, those would be simple to address, e.g. pass an ordinance that prohibits long-term operation of fossil fuel generators, defined as operating them for more than 40 days out of each 700. Or impose a reasonable decibel limit as measured at the property line.

Meanwhile people are proposing "data center moratoriums" and so on, even if the data center would be built in a non-residential area with a closed-loop cooling system and generate its power from a completely silent and non-polluting solar+battery system. Which makes it pretty obvious that the motivation is something else.

blocks.html_en: The whole thing is a motte and bailey game. The largest of cloud companies might build a data center which is 1GW. There won't be 4000 of those and it won't be next to your house. A huge number of commercial office buildings already have a 100kW data center in them, and have for decades, without anyone having any reason to know or care. For those 4000 is certainly an underestimate.

blocks.html_en: If all the talk is true that something approximating human intelligence can be scaled simply by building more datacenters, absolutely, it will be the most desired commodity in the world surpassing all others.

blocks.html_en:
> If all the talk is true that something approximating human intelligence can be scaled simply by building more datacenters.

How can an "approximation of human intelligence" be "scaled by building more datacenters"? That doesn't even make sense. You don't need more datacenters to demonstrate that you have an "approximation of human intelligence", all you need is a lab and at most 1 (one) datacenter. Only after you've demonstrated you know how to deliver the goods, you can think about scaling, retail and marketing.

The demand for the current "approximation" is out there for all to see, it's nothing like the "most desired commodity in the world", in fact, it needs huge inflationary subsidies in order to sustain some sort of demand-for-show.

sentences.en: > If all the talk is true that something approximating human intelligence can be scaled simply by building more datacenters.How can an "approximation of human intelligence" be "scaled by building more datacenters"?
sentences.zh: > 如果所有的说法都是正确的，即通过构建更多的数据中心就可以扩展近似人类智能的东西。那么“近似人类智能”如何“通过构建更多的数据中心来扩展”？

sentences.en: That doesn't even make sense.
sentences.zh: 这甚至没有意义。

sentences.en: You don't need more datacenters to demonstrate that you have an "approximation of human intelligence", all you need is a lab and at most 1 (one) datacenter.
sentences.zh: 您不需要更多的数据中心来证明您拥有“近似人类智能”，您所需要的只是一个实验室和最多一（一）个数据中心。

sentences.en: Only after you've demonstrated you know how to deliver the goods, you can think about scaling, retail and marketing.The demand for the current "approximation" is out there for all to see, it's nothing like the "most desired commodity in the world", in fact, it needs huge inflationary subsidies in order to sustain some sort of demand-for-show.
sentences.zh: 只有在你证明你知道如何交付货物之后，你才能考虑规模化、零售和营销。当前“近似值”的需求是有目共睹的，它与“世界上最需要的商品”完全不同，事实上，它需要巨额的通货膨胀补贴才能维持某种表演需求。

blocks.html_en:
> I think this is because the demand is so extreme for datacenters now

Point to _any_ analysis that says the current building boom is anything but speculation.

xAI is renting out their DC space. I think I read that Google is, too.

DC buildout is purely a gold rush. A play to be the one left standing after all the weaker players die.

blocks.html_en:
Speculation isn't a dirty word and both can be true.

Demand is very high today and exceeds capacity. Builders think demand will continue to grow. This seems plainly correct. Firms may differ in how high demand will go, but it is still more than today.

Whether or not the datacenters return on the investment is a different question.

blocks.html_en:
> That's what they should do. Datacenters don't need to be in anyone's backyard.

at least in texas that's what they are doing, building them out in the middle of nowhere or close to the middle of nowhere. The issue that I keep seeing pop up is behind the meter power generation ( gas turbines ) is hitting roadblock after roadblock. There just aren't very many and GE is scaling up but i mean it's not like adding RAM in AWS, it's going to take half a decade to see the increased inventory to buy one let alone onsite and operational.

"...demand from utilities, data centers and other power users continues to stretch manufacturing schedules into the next decade."

https://www.compressortech2.com/news/ge-vernova-raises-gas-t...

some are resorting to using ocean liner engines to generate electricity.

"That naturally explains the recent order flow into large reciprocating engines. In April, the Finnish vessel engine manufacturer Wärtsilä Oyj Abp announced a 790 MW off-grid power solution for a new Texas data centre facility,"

https://globalalphacapital.cclgroup.com/insight/gacm-from-se...

sentences.en: > That's what they should do.
sentences.zh: > 这就是他们应该做的。

sentences.en: Datacenters don't need to be in anyone's backyard.at least in texas that's what they are doing, building them out in the middle of nowhere or close to the middle of nowhere.
sentences.zh: 数据中心不需要建在任何人的后院。至少在德克萨斯州，他们就是这么做的，在偏僻的地方或接近偏僻的地方建造数据中心。

sentences.en: The issue that I keep seeing pop up is behind the meter power generation ( gas turbines ) is hitting roadblock after roadblock.
sentences.zh: 我不断看到的问题是仪表发电（燃气轮机）遇到了一个又一个的障碍。

sentences.en: There just aren't very many and GE is scaling up but i mean it's not like adding RAM in AWS, it's going to take half a decade to see the increased inventory to buy one let alone onsite and operational."...demand from utilities, data centers and other power users continues to stretch manufacturing schedules into the next decade."https://www.compressortech2.com/news/ge-vernova-raises-gas-t...some are resorting to using ocean liner engines to generate electricity."That naturally explains the recent order flow into large reciprocating engines.
sentences.zh: 只是数量不多，而且 GE 正在扩大规模，但我的意思是，这不像在 AWS 中添加 RAM，需要五年的时间才能看到库存增加，更不用说现场购买和运行了。“……来自公用事业、数据中心和其他电力用户的需求继续将制造时间表延长到下一个十年。”https://www.compressortech2.com/news/ge-vernova-raises-gas-t……有些人正在诉诸使用远洋客轮发动机来发电。“这自然解释了这一点最近的订单流向大型往复式发动机。

sentences.zh: 4 月，芬兰船舶发动机制造商 Wärtsilä Oyj Abp 宣布为德克萨斯州新数据中心设施提供 790 MW 离网电力解决方案，“https://globalalphacapital.cclgroup.com/insight/gacm-from-se...

blocks.html_en:
You're not getting it into an urban brownfield unless it comes with price controls on energy.

I live amongst the urban brownfields of Baltimore and our politicians are under tons of pressure to stop data center projects (and to tar and feather utility executives).

sentences.en: You're not getting it into an urban brownfield unless it comes with price controls on energy.I live amongst the urban brownfields of Baltimore and our politicians are under tons of pressure to stop data center projects (and to tar and feather utility executives).
sentences.zh: 除非对能源进行价格控制，否则你无法将其纳入城市棕地。我住在巴尔的摩的城市棕地中，我们的政客承受着巨大的压力，要求停止数据中心项目（以及焦油和羽毛公用事业高管）。

blocks.html_en:
>(and to tar and feather utility executives)

Has anyone thought about perhaps tar and feathering the politicans and bureaucrats that wrote all the rules that make greenfield development with a transmission line tap a non-starter?

The developers would looooove to not deal with brownfields, not have pissed off neighbors, not deal in complex urban sites, etc, but greenfield above a tiny size is effectively outlawed piecemeal in many states.

sentences.en: >(and to tar and feather utility executives)Has anyone thought about perhaps tar and feathering the politicans and bureaucrats that wrote all the rules that make greenfield development with a transmission line tap a non-starter?The developers would looooove to not deal with brownfields, not have pissed off neighbors, not deal in complex urban sites, etc, but greenfield above a tiny size is effectively outlawed piecemeal in many states.
sentences.zh: >（以及焦油和羽毛公用事业高管）有没有人想过也许焦油和羽化那些制定了所有规则的政治家和官僚，这些规则使输电线路的绿地开发成为不可能的事情？开发商会希望不处理棕地，不惹恼邻居，不处理复杂的城市场地等，但在许多州，规模以上的绿地实际上是被零碎地取缔的。

blocks.html_en:
I have some degree of sympathy for data centers, and anyone else that needs power, only because of how broken our regulatory state is on approving new transmission.

To be clear, it's not just data centers that have this problem. Journalists have been spilling digital ink on this topic as it relates to solar and wind farms as well. It's also part of the thing that'd hold back rapid deployment of nuclear.

If there's a strong enough economic incentive then yeah people are going to try to optimize around broken government.

sentences.zh: 我对数据中心和其他需要电力的人有一定程度的同情，只是因为我们的监管国家在批准新传输方面有多么糟糕。需要明确的是，不仅仅是数据中心存在这个问题。

sentences.en: Journalists have been spilling digital ink on this topic as it relates to solar and wind farms as well.
sentences.zh: 记者们一直在谈论这个话题，因为它也与太阳能和风电场有关。

sentences.en: It's also part of the thing that'd hold back rapid deployment of nuclear.If there's a strong enough economic incentive then yeah people are going to try to optimize around broken government.
sentences.zh: 这也是阻碍核能快速部署的部分原因。如果有足够强大的经济激励，那么人们就会尝试围绕破碎的政府进行优化。

blocks.html_en:
The country first and foremost should serve all its citizens. Not just wealthy people and not just people of the dominant ethnic background. The xAI data centers sits right next to a poor african american community and Musk used emergency turbines that were not designed for continuous daily use. He utilized a loophole to get past those restrictions. Those turbines are burning gasses inefficiently and producing a lot of pollution that is directly harming those residents who likely don't even materially benefit from all this effort.

These people likely had nothing to do with the delay in solar installs, or decades of nuclear NIMBYism yet now because Musk cannot fall behind, they now have to suffer with no one in government caring about them.

Its a real life hell, they cannot physically resist or cause any trouble or else the government will come and kill them where they stand, they cannot afford to move away because who would buy their houses (and why should they abandon their life's community for something they had no part in?) so they sit and suffer day after day with declining health for their family. Framed like this, Musk is like a locust who has come to devour whatever good there was and then move onto the next prey.

sentences.en: The country first and foremost should serve all its citizens.
sentences.zh: 国家首先应该为全体公民服务。

sentences.en: Not just wealthy people and not just people of the dominant ethnic background.
sentences.zh: 不仅仅是富人，也不仅仅是占主导地位的种族背景的人。

sentences.en: The xAI data centers sits right next to a poor african american community and Musk used emergency turbines that were not designed for continuous daily use.
sentences.zh: xAI 数据中心毗邻一个贫穷的非裔美国人社区，马斯克使用的紧急涡轮机并非为日常连续使用而设计。

sentences.en: He utilized a loophole to get past those restrictions.
sentences.zh: 他利用了一个漏洞来突破这些限制。

sentences.en: Those turbines are burning gasses inefficiently and producing a lot of pollution that is directly harming those residents who likely don't even materially benefit from all this effort.These people likely had nothing to do with the delay in solar installs, or decades of nuclear NIMBYism yet now because Musk cannot fall behind, they now have to suffer with no one in government caring about them.Its a real life hell, they cannot physically resist or cause any trouble or else the government will come and kill them where they stand, they cannot afford to move away because who would buy their houses (and why should they abandon their life's community for something they had no part in?)
sentences.zh: 这些涡轮机低效燃烧气体并产生大量污染，直接伤害了那些可能甚至没有从所有这些努力中获得实质利益的居民。这些人可能与太阳能安装的延误或数十年的核邻避主义无关，但现在因为马斯克不能落后，他们现在不得不在政府没有人关心他们的情况下受苦。这是现实生活中的地狱，他们无法身体抵抗或造成任何麻烦，否则政府会来把他们杀在原地，他们搬不起。离开是因为谁会买他们的房子（为什么他们要为了他们没有参与过的事情而放弃他们生活的社区？）

sentences.en: so they sit and suffer day after day with declining health for their family.
sentences.zh: 所以他们日复一日地坐着受苦，家人的健康状况每况愈下。

sentences.en: Framed like this, Musk is like a locust who has come to devour whatever good there was and then move onto the next prey.
sentences.zh: 在这样的框架下，马斯克就像一只蝗虫，它吞噬了一切美好的事物，然后转向下一个猎物。

blocks.html_en: I can attest for the smells better. Now, I'm left wondering which smells worse, an AI datacenter or a large beer brewery. As a kid, we had a Mrs. Baird's bread bakery which was in the heart of town that students would take field trips to see (which is no longer there and is now apartments). On the other side of town is a Budweiser plant, and it's infamous for being so odorific but it is located way the hell out there.

blocks.html_en:
I used to work nextdoor to the broadcast headquarters to an old sports cable network and their satellite uplinks. They had several diesel generators on site to ensure power never went down else their entire network went dark. If power was lost, they had batteries for immediate power that lasted long enough for the generators to kick in. When the generators kicked in, it went from being a nice quiet area to being in the parking lot of a truck stop with all of the engines running. If these new datacenters are powering themselves with generators, then yeah, I'd not want them to be around me either.

I think your ire at the discussion is pointed in the wrong direction.

sentences.en: I used to work nextdoor to the broadcast headquarters to an old sports cable network and their satellite uplinks.
sentences.zh: 我曾经在广播总部旁边工作，旁边有一个旧的体育有线网络及其卫星上行链路。

sentences.en: They had several diesel generators on site to ensure power never went down else their entire network went dark.
sentences.zh: 他们现场有几台柴油发电机，以确保电力永远不会中断，否则整个网络都会陷入黑暗。

sentences.en: If power was lost, they had batteries for immediate power that lasted long enough for the generators to kick in.
sentences.zh: 如果断电，他们有电池可以立即供电，持续时间足以让发电机启动。

sentences.en: When the generators kicked in, it went from being a nice quiet area to being in the parking lot of a truck stop with all of the engines running.
sentences.zh: 当发电机启动时，这里从一个安静的区域变成了一个卡车停靠站的停车场，所有发动机都在运转。

sentences.en: If these new datacenters are powering themselves with generators, then yeah, I'd not want them to be around me either.I think your ire at the discussion is pointed in the wrong direction.
sentences.zh: 如果这些新的数据中心用发电机为自己供电，那么是的，我也不希望它们在我周围。我认为你对讨论的愤怒指向了错误的方向。

blocks.html_en:
I agree that I don’t want a data center in my backyard either.

What’s missing from this strange conversation is discussion about how many data center proposals are near residential areas.

I tried to look for one in my state and a few surrounding areas and couldn’t find any. There was an amusing story about how data center protestors had to organize expensive bus trips to get the protesters out to the data center site because it was so remote.

There was another story about a small town without any major electricity or communication infrastructure coming together to impose a moratorium on data center construction. There were no plans to build data centers in their small town and no reason to consider it at all, but they felt so threatened that they needed to convene and put on a big show about banning it.

The social phenomenon of the data center construction protest movement is really fascinating. My assumption is that it will run its course and then be mostly dropped when the next current thing comes along.

There are some actual concerns about using natural gas instead of trying to utilize the optimal power sources or locations, but they’re mostly overshadowed by bogeyman complaints like thinking that a data center is going to end up in your backyard or water consumption that is negligible compared to golf courses and swimming pools.

sentences.en: I agree that I don’t want a data center in my backyard either.What’s missing from this strange conversation is discussion about how many data center proposals are near residential areas.I tried to look for one in my state and a few surrounding areas and couldn’t find any.
sentences.zh: 我同意我也不希望在我的后院建立数据中心。这场奇怪的谈话中缺少的是关于有多少个数据中心提案位于住宅区附近的讨论。我试图在我所在的州和一些周边地区寻找一个，但没有找到。

sentences.en: There was an amusing story about how data center protestors had to organize expensive bus trips to get the protesters out to the data center site because it was so remote.There was another story about a small town without any major electricity or communication infrastructure coming together to impose a moratorium on data center construction.
sentences.zh: 有一个有趣的故事是关于数据中心抗议者如何组织昂贵的巴士旅行才能将抗议者带到数据中心站点，因为它是如此偏远。还有另一个故事是关于一个没有任何主要电力或通信基础设施的小镇聚集在一起暂停数据中心建设。

sentences.en: There were no plans to build data centers in their small town and no reason to consider it at all, but they felt so threatened that they needed to convene and put on a big show about banning it.The social phenomenon of the data center construction protest movement is really fascinating.
sentences.zh: 他们的小镇没有建设数据中心的计划，也没有理由考虑，但他们感到受到了很大的威胁，所以需要召开一场关于禁止数据中心的大秀。数据中心建设抗议运动的社会现象确实令人着迷。

sentences.en: My assumption is that it will run its course and then be mostly dropped when the next current thing comes along.There are some actual concerns about using natural gas instead of trying to utilize the optimal power sources or locations, but they’re mostly overshadowed by bogeyman complaints like thinking that a data center is going to end up in your backyard or water consumption that is negligible compared to golf courses and swimming pools.

> There were no plans to build data centers in their small town and no reason to consider it at all

You have to be fair on this though. The time to ban something is before they start talking about doing it, not after. Being ahead of the game is never a bad thing, especially when small towns are so easily exploited due to income and infrastructure. They just can't afford long legal battles.

blocks.html_en:
I'd like to add some extra strong emphasis/support to your post:

>Being ahead of the game is never a bad thing, especially when small towns are so easily exploited due to income and infrastructure. They just can't afford long legal battles.

It's not merely "never a bad thing", for certain tools like zoning it is, at least in the jurisdictions I know of, required to be well ahead and consistently enforced. It's not something towns are allowed to do retroactively, if you have no proper legal town plan/zoning setup in place as well as various ordinances like noise then by default the only controls are the relatively weak state ones. There are many things a government is allowed to do if it's part of an overall plan that applies to all equally, but not if it's "we just don't like those guys specifically", and the best way to demonstrate that is to be making no special moves around a given arrival at all but rather to have it all setup.

Of course, there are as you suggest lots of other good reasons to be ahead too. It's a lot easier to consider things thoroughly and get community agreement when there isn't massive time pressure or lobbying going on and emotions aren't running high. It's easier to get some expert consultation at reasonable prices (in some cases there may even be community members with the skills and willingness to volunteer if it's not a time crush), to enact longer term infrastructure plans, to get any state approval if needed, etc etc. But there are legally serious hard lines as well and if towns are caught napping they may just not be able to use them at all.

sentences.en: I'd like to add some extra strong emphasis/support to your post:>Being ahead of the game is never a bad thing, especially when small towns are so easily exploited due to income and infrastructure.
sentences.zh: 我想在你的帖子中添加一些额外的强调/支持：>领先从来都不是一件坏事，特别是当小镇由于收入和基础设施而很容易被利用时。

sentences.en: They just can't afford long legal battles.It's not merely "never a bad thing", for certain tools like zoning it is, at least in the jurisdictions I know of, required to be well ahead and consistently enforced.
sentences.zh: 他们只是无法承受长期的法律斗争。这不仅仅是“从来都不是一件坏事”，对于某些工具（例如分区）来说，至少在我所知道的司法管辖区中，需要领先并始终如一地执行。

sentences.en: It's not something towns are allowed to do retroactively, if you have no proper legal town plan/zoning setup in place as well as various ordinances like noise then by default the only controls are the relatively weak state ones.
sentences.zh: 这不是城镇被允许追溯的事情，如果你没有适当的合法城镇规划/分区设置以及噪音等各种法令，那么默认情况下，唯一的控制措施是相对较弱的州控制措施。

sentences.en: There are many things a government is allowed to do if it's part of an overall plan that applies to all equally, but not if it's "we just don't like those guys specifically", and the best way to demonstrate that is to be making no special moves around a given arrival at all but rather to have it all setup.Of course, there are as you suggest lots of other good reasons to be ahead too.
sentences.zh: 如果政府是平等适用于所有人的总体计划的一部分，那么政府可以做很多事情，但如果是“我们只是不喜欢那些人”，那就不行了，证明这一点的最好方法就是对特定的抵达完全不采取任何特殊行动，而是将其全部设置好。当然，正如您所建议的，还有很多其他很好的理由来领先。

sentences.en: It's a lot easier to consider things thoroughly and get community agreement when there isn't massive time pressure or lobbying going on and emotions aren't running high.
sentences.zh: 当没有大量时间压力或进行游说且情绪不高时，更容易彻底考虑问题并获得社区一致同意。

sentences.en: It's easier to get some expert consultation at reasonable prices (in some cases there may even be community members with the skills and willingness to volunteer if it's not a time crush), to enact longer term infrastructure plans, to get any state approval if needed, etc etc. But there are legally serious hard lines as well and if towns are caught napping they may just not be able to use them at all.
sentences.zh: 以合理的价格获得专家咨询（在某些情况下，如果不是时间紧迫的话，甚至可能有具备技能且愿意自愿参与的社区成员）、制定长期基础设施计划、在需要时获得国家批准等，等等。但法律上也有严格的强硬路线，如果城镇被抓到打瞌睡，他们可能根本无法使用它们。

sentences.zh: 我想在你的帖子中添加一些额外的强调/支持：>领先从来都不是一件坏事，特别是当小镇由于收入和基础设施而很容易被利用时。

sentences.en: They just can't afford long legal battles.It's not merely "never a bad thing", for certain tools like zoning it is, at least in the jurisdictions I know of, required to be well ahead and consistently enforced.
sentences.zh: 他们只是无法承受长期的法律斗争。这不仅仅是“从来都不是一件坏事”，对于某些工具（例如分区）来说，至少在我所知道的司法管辖区中，需要领先并始终如一地执行。

sentences.en: It's not something towns are allowed to do retroactively, if you have no proper legal town plan/zoning setup in place as well as various ordinances like noise then by default the only controls are the relatively weak state ones.
sentences.zh: 这不是城镇被允许追溯的事情，如果你没有适当的合法城镇规划/分区设置以及噪音等各种法令，那么默认情况下，唯一的控制措施是相对较弱的州控制措施。

sentences.en: There are many things a government is allowed to do if it's part of an overall plan that applies to all equally, but not if it's "we just don't like those guys specifically", and the best way to demonstrate that is to be making no special moves around a given arrival at all but rather to have it all setup.Of course, there are as you suggest lots of other good reasons to be ahead too.
sentences.zh: 如果政府是平等适用于所有人的总体计划的一部分，那么政府可以做很多事情，但如果是“我们只是不喜欢那些人”，那就不行了，证明这一点的最好方法就是对特定的抵达完全不采取任何特殊行动，而是将其全部设置好。当然，正如您所建议的，还有很多其他很好的理由来领先。

sentences.en: It's a lot easier to consider things thoroughly and get community agreement when there isn't massive time pressure or lobbying going on and emotions aren't running high.
sentences.zh: 当没有大量时间压力或进行游说且情绪不高时，更容易彻底考虑问题并获得社区一致同意。

sentences.en: It's easier to get some expert consultation at reasonable prices (in some cases there may even be community members with the skills and willingness to volunteer if it's not a time crush), to enact longer term infrastructure plans, to get any state approval if needed, etc etc. But there are legally serious hard lines as well and if towns are caught napping they may just not be able to use them at all.
sentences.zh: 以合理的价格获得专家咨询（在某些情况下，如果不是时间紧迫的话，甚至可能有具备技能且愿意自愿参与的社区成员）、制定长期基础设施计划、在需要时获得国家批准等，等等。但法律上也有严格的强硬路线，如果城镇被抓到打瞌睡，他们可能根本无法使用它们。

The interesting thing about the anecdote wasn't the timing. It was that there was no reason to build a data center there but they felt compelled to make it their top priority.

> especially when small towns are so easily exploited due to income and infrastructure.

Small, remote towns without electricity or data connections are not easily exploited for data centers.

I don't think anyone's talking about towns where electricity is absent or expensive.

Instead it's locations that begin with cheap electricity (attracting the datacenter) but the residents fear a significant increase in electricity costs with no local economic benefits to offset it.

"I see you're complaining about society and yet you participate in society! Very interesting! I am very smart."

This is not a good or reasoned take. People organize where the other people are because that's how you get the most people. It is nor hypocrisy to use Facebook as a platform to organize protests against other tech companies. Just as it is not hypocrisy to criticize the society you live within.

This is a very stupid observation made by people who can't be bothered to consider the realities of life away from their keyboard.

blocks.html_en: It is a good or reasoned take (but perhaps not both!). There is some sliver of hypocrisy in condemning tech companies wholesale while enjoying their products. But just because something is hypocritical, it doesn't invalidate all related criticisms. If you don't like the negative byproducts of a good or service, that's a perfectly valid gripe. But those byproducts are a necessary consequence of the status quo. In order to eliminate or mitigate the undesired consequences, some change would need to occur in the real world that would require a hitherto unforeseen innovation or it would otherwise increase the cost of providing the good or service, potentially so much so that prices would increase to undesirable levels, or it would become economically unviable to produce the good or service entirely. So without more specific criticism, and combined with polemic rhetoric, it's reasonable to assume that some are arguing that the world would be better off without AI datacenters and the models they support entirely. And if those same persons enjoy those same services, it's reasonable to sense some tension there. Maybe they haven't thought the whole thing through. I don't think it's a stupid observation.

sentences.en: It is a good or reasoned take (but perhaps not both!).
sentences.zh: 这是一个好的或合理的看法（但也许不是两者兼而有之！）。

sentences.en: There is some sliver of hypocrisy in condemning tech companies wholesale while enjoying their products.
sentences.zh: 在享受科技公司产品的同时，对其进行大规模谴责，这多少有些虚伪。

sentences.en: But just because something is hypocritical, it doesn't invalidate all related criticisms.
sentences.zh: 但仅仅因为某件事是虚伪的，并不意味着所有相关的批评都无效。

sentences.en: If you don't like the negative byproducts of a good or service, that's a perfectly valid gripe.
sentences.zh: 如果你不喜欢某种商品或服务的负面副产品，那么这是一个完全合理的抱怨。

sentences.en: But those byproducts are a necessary consequence of the status quo.
sentences.zh: 但这些副产品是现状的必然结果。

sentences.en: In order to eliminate or mitigate the undesired consequences, some change would need to occur in the real world that would require a hitherto unforeseen innovation or it would otherwise increase the cost of providing the good or service, potentially so much so that prices would increase to undesirable levels, or it would become economically unviable to produce the good or service entirely.
sentences.zh: 为了消除或减轻不良后果，现实世界需要发生一些变化，这需要迄今为止无法预见的创新，否则会增加提供商品或服务的成本，有可能导致价格上涨到不希望的水平，或者完全生产商品或服务在经济上变得不可行。

sentences.en: So without more specific criticism, and combined with polemic rhetoric, it's reasonable to assume that some are arguing that the world would be better off without AI datacenters and the models they support entirely.
sentences.zh: 因此，如果没有更具体的批评，再加上争论性的言论，我们可以合理地假设一些人认为，如果没有人工智能数据中心及其完全支持的模型，世界会变得更好。

sentences.en: And if those same persons enjoy those same services, it's reasonable to sense some tension there.
sentences.zh: 如果这些人享受同样的服务，那么感觉到一些紧张也是合理的。

sentences.en: Maybe they haven't thought the whole thing through.
sentences.zh: 也许他们还没有把整个事情想清楚。

sentences.en: I don't think it's a stupid observation.
sentences.zh: 我不认为这是一个愚蠢的观察。

blocks.html_en: It is a good or reasoned take (but perhaps not both!). There is some sliver of hypocrisy in condemning tech companies wholesale while enjoying their products. But just because something is hypocritical, it doesn't invalidate all related criticisms. If you don't like the negative byproducts of a good or service, that's a perfectly valid gripe. But those byproducts are a necessary consequence of the status quo. In order to eliminate or mitigate the undesired consequences, some change would need to occur in the real world that would require a hitherto unforeseen innovation or it would otherwise increase the cost of providing the good or service, potentially so much so that prices would increase to undesirable levels, or it would become economically unviable to produce the good or service entirely. So without more specific criticism, and combined with polemic rhetoric, it's reasonable to assume that some are arguing that the world would be better off without AI datacenters and the models they support entirely. And if those same persons enjoy those same services, it's reasonable to sense some tension there. Maybe they haven't thought the whole thing through. I don't think it's a stupid observation.

blocks.html_en: Yea, there's probably not one single issue that people are fed up with, but datacenters have become lightning rods that represent big tech's disproportionate power over us, job-displacing AI, and are a visible example of how municipalities that we elect--serve and prioritize companies from out of town over their voters.

sentences.en: Yea, there's probably not one single issue that people are fed up with, but datacenters have become lightning rods that represent big tech's disproportionate power over us, job-displacing AI, and are a visible example of how municipalities that we elect--serve and prioritize companies from out of town over their voters.
sentences.zh: 是的，人们可能不会厌倦任何一个问题，但数据中心已经成为避雷针，代表着大型科技对我们的不成比例的权力，取代工作的人工智能，并且是我们选举的市政当局如何服务和优先考虑外地公司而不是选民的明显例子。

blocks.html_en:
I wouldn't want a GPU datacenter close to my house either.

They should buy land in middle of nowhere, many miles from any closest town, no less than 5. And they should build and provide their own electricity.

I don't see any other solution to make everyone coexist in peace.

Imagine it was your house a mile from such a monster. Eyesore, noisy.

sentences.en: I wouldn't want a GPU datacenter close to my house either.They should buy land in middle of nowhere, many miles from any closest town, no less than 5.
sentences.zh: 我也不希望 GPU 数据中心靠近我家。他们应该在偏僻的地方购买土地，距离最近的城镇很远，不少于 5 英里。

sentences.en: And they should build and provide their own electricity.I don't see any other solution to make everyone coexist in peace.Imagine it was your house a mile from such a monster.
sentences.zh: 他们应该建造并提供自己的电力。我看不到任何其他解决方案可以让每个人和平共处。想象一下，这是你的房子，距离这样一个怪物一英里。

sentences.en: Eyesore, noisy.
sentences.zh: 碍眼，吵闹。

blocks.html_en:
> What’s missing from this strange conversation is discussion about how many data center proposals are near residential areas.

What's strange is that you didn't once mention the layperson's fear of data center projects in their county/state potentially raising the cost of consumer electricity. I've never heard a layperson mention data centers without mentioning that fear.

Have you really heard zero occurrences of that fear in your view-from-Mars survey of this issue?

sentences.zh: > 这次奇怪的谈话中缺少的是关于有多少个数据中心提案位于住宅区附近的讨论。奇怪的是，您一次没有提到外行人担心其县/州的数据中心项目可能会增加消费者的电力成本。

sentences.en: I've never heard a layperson mention data centers without mentioning that fear.Have you really heard zero occurrences of that fear in your view-from-Mars survey of this issue?
sentences.zh: 我从来没有听过外行人提到数据中心而不提到这种恐惧。在您对这个问题的火星观察调查中，您真的听说过这种恐惧的发生率为零吗？

blocks.html_en:
And on the flip side, I live in Fairfax County, VA and there are data centers EVERYWHERE. Especially if I cross into Ashburn (home of AOL and stuff) - literally mixed in with large townhome developments.

And the current plan - building a data center in Canaan Valley - is a whopper... 785 MW natural gas power plant on site with diesel generator backups (solely for the data center, not sold back to grid), 500+ acres of land, 2 million sqft initial building space, air quality impact assessment by the state is heavily redacted, $4 billion price tag.

All right next to one of DC's most popular outdoor/skiing recreation areas. The DC would loom over the two towns that support the entire region.

sentences.en: And on the flip side, I live in Fairfax County, VA and there are data centers EVERYWHERE.
sentences.zh: 另一方面，我住在弗吉尼亚州费尔法克斯县，到处都有数据中心。

sentences.en: Especially if I cross into Ashburn (home of AOL and stuff) - literally mixed in with large townhome developments.And the current plan - building a data center in Canaan Valley - is a whopper...
sentences.zh: 特别是如果我进入阿什本（AOL 等公司的所在地）——实际上与大型联排别墅开发项目混在一起。而目前的计划——在迦南谷建立一个数据中心——是一个巨大的......

sentences.en: 785 MW natural gas power plant on site with diesel generator backups (solely for the data center, not sold back to grid), 500+ acres of land, 2 million sqft initial building space, air quality impact assessment by the state is heavily redacted, $4 billion price tag.All right next to one of DC's most popular outdoor/skiing recreation areas.
sentences.zh: 现场配备 785 兆瓦天然气发电厂，配有备用柴油发电机（仅用于数据中心，不卖回电网）、500 多英亩土地、200 万平方英尺初始建筑空间、州空气质量影响评估经过严格修订，标价 40 亿美元。一切都在华盛顿最受欢迎的户外/滑雪休闲区之一旁边。

sentences.en: The DC would loom over the two towns that support the entire region.
sentences.zh: 华盛顿特区将笼罩在支持整个地区的两个城镇上空。

blocks.html_en: And the current plan - building a data center in Canaan Valley - is a whopper... 785 MW natural gas power plant on site with diesel generator backups (solely for the data center, not sold back to grid), 500+ acres of land, 2 million sqft initial building space, air quality impact assessment by the state is heavily redacted, $4 billion price tag.

sentences.en: And the current plan - building a data center in Canaan Valley - is a whopper...
sentences.zh: 目前的计划——在迦南谷建设一个数据中心——是一个巨大的计划……

sentences.en: 785 MW natural gas power plant on site with diesel generator backups (solely for the data center, not sold back to grid), 500+ acres of land, 2 million sqft initial building space, air quality impact assessment by the state is heavily redacted, $4 billion price tag.
sentences.zh: 现场 785 兆瓦天然气发电厂，配有备用柴油发电机（仅用于数据中心，不卖回电网），500 多英亩土地，200 万平方英尺初始建筑空间，经过严格修订的州空气质量影响评估，标价 40 亿美元。

blocks.html_en: All right next to one of DC's most popular outdoor/skiing recreation areas. The DC would loom over the two towns that support the entire region.

sentences.en: All right next to one of DC's most popular outdoor/skiing recreation areas.
sentences.zh: 就在华盛顿最受欢迎的户外/滑雪休闲区之一旁边。

sentences.en: The DC would loom over the two towns that support the entire region.
sentences.zh: 华盛顿特区将笼罩在支持整个地区的两个城镇上空。

blocks.html_en:
>not sold back to grid)

They'd probably love to sell spare capacity back to the grid but the way interconnection is handled they'd likely be on the hook for a bunch of other upgrades to facilitate that hence why they're not.

You can't "just" add generation to the capacity to the grid without $5-15mil (could be way more but that'll usually torpedo a project) of cost.

sentences.en: >not sold back to grid)They'd probably love to sell spare capacity back to the grid but the way interconnection is handled they'd likely be on the hook for a bunch of other upgrades to facilitate that hence why they're not.You can't "just" add generation to the capacity to the grid without $5-15mil (could be way more but that'll usually torpedo a project) of cost.
sentences.zh: >不卖回电网）他们可能很乐意将闲置容量卖回电网，但处理互连的方式可能会让他们陷入一堆其他升级的困境，以促进这一点，因此他们不这样做。你不能“仅仅”在没有5-1500万美元（可能更多，但这通常会破坏一个项目）的成本的情况下将发电量添加到电网中。

blocks.html_en:
It also doesn't necessarily matter if the data centers actually raise electricity rates.

Regardless of whether or not they do, you can block their construction and try to horse-trade it for relief on energy prices.

blocks.html_en:
>It also doesn't necessarily matter if the data centers actually raise electricity rates.

>Regardless of whether or not they do, you can block their construction and try to horse-trade it for relief on energy prices.

So in other words burn the credibility and reputation of whatever you're using for that purpose so you can pull a quick "fuck you got mine" to the tune of several cents per kwh off your bill.

That sort of policy making is not sustainable and is exactly the sort of selfish shortsighted thinking that birthed the current political climate.

Edit: At leas you're honest about it. The usual peddlers of such stuff are in denial about what they're doing so they can't be made to understand the tradeoffs.

sentences.en: >It also doesn't necessarily matter if the data centers actually raise electricity rates.>Regardless of whether or not they do, you can block their construction and try to horse-trade it for relief on energy prices.So in other words burn the credibility and reputation of whatever you're using for that purpose so you can pull a quick "fuck you got mine" to the tune of several cents per kwh off your bill.That sort of policy making is not sustainable and is exactly the sort of selfish shortsighted thinking that birthed the current political climate.Edit: At leas you're honest about it.
sentences.zh: >数据中心是否真的提高电费也不一定重要。>无论他们是否这样做，你都可以阻止他们的建设，并试图通过讨价还价来降低能源价格。换句话说，烧毁你为此目的使用的任何东西的可信度和声誉，这样你就可以快速地说一句“操，你得到了我的”，每千瓦时的电价减少了几美分。这种政策制定是不可持续的，而且正是那种自私的短视思维，催生了当前的政治Climate.Edit：至少你对此是诚实的。

sentences.en: The usual peddlers of such stuff are in denial about what they're doing so they can't be made to understand the tradeoffs.
sentences.zh: 此类东西的通常小贩都否认自己正在做的事情，因此无法让他们理解其中的权衡。

blocks.html_en: As I see it, the AI companies are the ones that started the "fuck you got mine" defense. Look at how they obtained their data to train their models. Look at how they've gone about building their data centers. It's yet another example of how tech companies hurry up to get established with no regards for anything other than getting to a point of being too big to stop.

sentences.en: As I see it, the AI companies are the ones that started the "fuck you got mine" defense.
sentences.zh: 在我看来，人工智能公司是那些开始“操你妈的我的”防御的公司。

sentences.en: Look at how they obtained their data to train their models.
sentences.zh: 看看他们如何获取数据来训练模型。

sentences.en: Look at how they've gone about building their data centers.
sentences.zh: 看看他们是如何构建数据中心的。

sentences.en: It's yet another example of how tech companies hurry up to get established with no regards for anything other than getting to a point of being too big to stop.
sentences.zh: 这是科技公司匆忙成立的又一个例子，除了发展到大到无法停止的地步之外，不考虑任何其他事情。

blocks.html_en:
I can't speak for elsewhere, but a town near me has a few data centers, and the anti-datacenter sentiment precedes their use for LLMs.

They simply take a lot of space, and the city gave sweet tax deals, and they employ almost no one. The locals aren't happy with that type of land use.

sentences.en: I can't speak for elsewhere, but a town near me has a few data centers, and the anti-datacenter sentiment precedes their use for LLMs.They simply take a lot of space, and the city gave sweet tax deals, and they employ almost no one.
sentences.zh: 我不能代表其他地方，但我附近的一个城镇有几个数据中心，而反数据中心的情绪先于法学硕士使用它们。它们只是占用了大量空间，而且该市提供了优惠的税收优惠，而且他们几乎不雇用任何人。

sentences.en: The locals aren't happy with that type of land use.
sentences.zh: 当地人对这种土地利用方式并不满意。

blocks.html_en:
Most of it is anti-AI sentiment, there are already a ton of people who have lost jobs, or clients to AI.

What bothers me is clearing forests, sometimes even protected forests with the idea that they'll ask for forgiveness instead of permission.

sentences.en: Most of it is anti-AI sentiment, there are already a ton of people who have lost jobs, or clients to AI.What bothers me is clearing forests, sometimes even protected forests with the idea that they'll ask for forgiveness instead of permission.
sentences.zh: 其中大部分是反人工智能情绪，已经有很多人失去了工作或人工智能的客户。令我困扰的是砍伐森林，有时甚至是受保护的森林，因为他们会请求宽恕而不是许可。

If you look at Elk Grove Village, Illinois as their budget there after declaring they would become the AI Datacenter Capital of the World -- there is an obvious answer:

Suburbs cannot continue to pay for their infrastructure without drastically increasing taxes or finding new sources of income.

blocks.html_en:
Data centers are conflated with rising energy costs.

It's partly about people's nominal objections, but mostly an attempt to hold them hostage to extract some energy bill subsidies (and general cost of living relief).

sentences.en: Data centers are conflated with rising energy costs.It's partly about people's nominal objections, but mostly an attempt to hold them hostage to extract some energy bill subsidies (and general cost of living relief).
sentences.zh: 数据中心与不断上涨的能源成本混为一谈。部分原因是人们名义上的反对，但主要是试图将他们作为人质，以获取一些能源账单补贴（以及一般生活成本减免）。

Hacker News guidelines:

"Be kind. Don't be snarky. Converse curiously; don't cross-examine. Edit out swipes.

Comments should get more thoughtful and substantive, not less, as a topic gets more divisive.

When disagreeing, please reply to the argument instead of calling names. "That is idiotic; 1 + 1 is 2, not 3" can be shortened to "1 + 1 is 2, not 3.""

sentences.en: Hacker News guidelines:"Be kind.
sentences.zh: 黑客新闻指南：“保持友善。

sentences.en: Don't be snarky.
sentences.zh: 别尖酸刻薄。

sentences.en: Converse curiously; don't cross-examine.
sentences.zh: 好奇地交谈；不要盘问。

sentences.en: Edit out swipes.Comments should get more thoughtful and substantive, not less, as a topic gets more divisive.When disagreeing, please reply to the argument instead of calling names.

blocks.html_en:
No one ever cared about DCs before now. I think there’s a lot of social media influence from nation state competitors going on to try and slow SOTA development in the US.

Ironically, There’s enough issues with the DC buildout going on that even with 100% public opinion support it would still be significantly delayed. The behind the meter power generation isn’t going well. There’s only so many gas fired turbines on the market. And connecting a DC to an established grid isn’t like clicking a button in AWS no matter how much money you have.

sentences.en: No one ever cared about DCs before now.
sentences.zh: 在此之前没有人关心过 DC。

sentences.en: I think there’s a lot of social media influence from nation state competitors going on to try and slow SOTA development in the US.Ironically, There’s enough issues with the DC buildout going on that even with 100% public opinion support it would still be significantly delayed.
sentences.zh: 我认为来自民族国家竞争对手的社交媒体影响力很大，试图减缓美国 SOTA 的发展。具有讽刺意味的是，DC 建设存在足够多的问题，即使有 100% 的公众舆论支持，它仍然会被严重推迟。

sentences.en: The behind the meter power generation isn’t going well.
sentences.zh: 电表后面的发电进展并不顺利。

sentences.en: There’s only so many gas fired turbines on the market.
sentences.zh: 市场上的燃气轮机数量有限。

sentences.en: And connecting a DC to an established grid isn’t like clicking a button in AWS no matter how much money you have.
sentences.zh: 无论您有多少钱，将数据中心连接到已建立的电网并不像单击 AWS 中的按钮一样。

blocks.html_en:
> I think there’s a lot of social media influence from nation state competitors going on to try and slow SOTA development in the US.

This comes cross as unbelievable, and at worst a fantastical statement, as someone who lives in Northern Virginia and is effectively watching the Datacenter battle unfold from the front lines, and dealing with the local politics and electricity costs rising at a precipitous rate.

Our electricity bill has doubled. Loudoun County Board of Supervisors want to erect “Statue of Liberty” sized power transmission lines, and those that want data centers are doing their damndest to somehow claim that they’re not responsible for the rise in the cost of utilities and shouldn’t be held responsible for it.

Part of the reason people care about Datacenters now is that they’re starting to litter the landscape, take resources from local municipalities, and raise costs for everyone involved, whereas previously there were not enough of them to really move the needle as being a problem.

Folks care because their bottom line is impacted in a time when it’s already hard to make ends meet every month due to rising costs of food, gas, and utilities, not because of some unnamed boogeyman in the form of a “nation state competitor”.

sentences.en: > I think there’s a lot of social media influence from nation state competitors going on to try and slow SOTA development in the US.This comes cross as unbelievable, and at worst a fantastical statement, as someone who lives in Northern Virginia and is effectively watching the Datacenter battle unfold from the front lines, and dealing with the local politics and electricity costs rising at a precipitous rate.Our electricity bill has doubled.
sentences.zh: > 我认为来自民族国家竞争对手的社交媒体影响力很大，试图减缓美国 SOTA 的发展。对于住在弗吉尼亚州北部并在前线有效观察数据中心之战的人来说，这是令人难以置信的，在最坏的情况下是一个梦幻般的声明，并处理当地政治和急剧上升的电力成本。我们的电费增加了一倍。

sentences.en: Loudoun County Board of Supervisors want to erect “Statue of Liberty” sized power transmission lines, and those that want data centers are doing their damndest to somehow claim that they’re not responsible for the rise in the cost of utilities and shouldn’t be held responsible for it.Part of the reason people care about Datacenters now is that they’re starting to litter the landscape, take resources from local municipalities, and raise costs for everyone involved, whereas previously there were not enough of them to really move the needle as being a problem.Folks care because their bottom line is impacted in a time when it’s already hard to make ends meet every month due to rising costs of food, gas, and utilities, not because of some unnamed boogeyman in the form of a “nation state competitor”.
sentences.zh: 劳登县监事会希望架设“自由女神像”大小的输电线路，而那些想要数据中心的人正在竭尽全力地声称他们对公用事业成本的上涨不负有责任，也不应该为此承担责任。人们现在关心数据中心的部分原因是，他们开始乱扔垃圾，从当地市政当局夺取资源，并提高了每个相关人员的成本，而以前没有足够多的数据中心来真正推动这一目标。人们之所以关心这个问题，是因为他们的底线受到了影响，因为食品、天然气和公用事业成本不断上涨，每个月都已经难以收支平衡，而不是因为某个“民族国家竞争对手”形式的无名恶人。

blocks.html_en:
> I think there’s a lot of social media influence from nation state competitors going on to try and slow SOTA development in the US.

This comes cross as unbelievable, and at worst a fantastical statement, as someone who lives in Northern Virginia and is effectively watching the Datacenter battle unfold from the front lines, and dealing with the local politics and electricity costs rising at a precipitous rate.

Our electricity bill has doubled. Loudoun County Board of Supervisors want to erect “Statue of Liberty” sized power transmission lines, and those that want data centers are doing their damndest to somehow claim that they’re not responsible for the rise in the cost of utilities and shouldn’t be held responsible for it.

Part of the reason people care about Datacenters now is that they’re starting to litter the landscape, take resources from local municipalities, and raise costs for everyone involved, whereas previously there were not enough of them to really move the needle as being a problem.

Folks care because their bottom line is impacted in a time when it’s already hard to make ends meet every month due to rising costs of food, gas, and utilities, not because of some unnamed boogeyman in the form of a “nation state competitor”.

blocks.html_en:
They're building a mass of DC's near me as well in northern IL (not quite as many as by you). I see a lot of weird stuff on social media - people inactive for years all of a sudden becoming super-activists and posting non-stop anti-DC content. Nearly identical content to what's being pushed by a bunch of faceless accounts named like "Water4All","Nature Reign" - something vaguely environmental sounding.

Talking points like:

"We should call data centers what they actually are: surveillance centers designed to put us all in a digital prison by 2030"

It's fishy. People aren't quite showing up and saying the same things at city council meetings, but the local city anti-DC group is full of people reposting this stuff. One clickbait article in particular, "there's no such thing as a closed loop system".

There's real concerns with the data centers. But, the opposition to them doesn't feel entirely organic (imo).

sentences.en: They're building a mass of DC's near me as well in northern IL (not quite as many as by you).
sentences.zh: 他们正在我附近以及伊利诺伊州北部建造大量的 DC（没有你的那么多）。

sentences.en: I see a lot of weird stuff on social media - people inactive for years all of a sudden becoming super-activists and posting non-stop anti-DC content.
sentences.zh: 我在社交媒体上看到很多奇怪的东西——多年不活跃的人们突然变成超级活动分子，并不停地发布反 DC 内容。

sentences.en: Nearly identical content to what's being pushed by a bunch of faceless accounts named like "Water4All","Nature Reign" - something vaguely environmental sounding.Talking points like:"We should call data centers what they actually are: surveillance centers designed to put us all in a digital prison by 2030"It's fishy.
sentences.zh: 内容与一群名为“Water4All”、“Nature Reign”等不知名账户所推送的内容几乎相同——听上去有些模糊。诸如此类的谈话要点：“我们应该以数据中心的实际名称来称呼它们：旨在到 2030 年将我们所有人关进数字监狱的监控中心”，这很可疑。

sentences.en: People aren't quite showing up and saying the same things at city council meetings, but the local city anti-DC group is full of people reposting this stuff.
sentences.zh: 人们并没有在市议会会议上出现并说同样的话，但当地的城市反 DC 团体却到处都是转发这些内容的人。

sentences.zh: 特别是一篇标题党文章，“不存在闭环系统这样的东西”。数据中心确实令人担忧。

sentences.en: But, the opposition to them doesn't feel entirely organic (imo).
sentences.zh: 但是，对他们的反对感觉并不完全是有机的（我认为）。

blocks.html_en:
"there's no such thing as a closed loop system"

That's not incorrect though; you're still needing to shed the heat energy SOMEWHERE. Thermodynamics alone dictates that basic fact. You may not waste the cooling solution but all that excessive GPU heat is going to need to go somewhere - and the only place it ultimately goes is right into the environment.

Ever stand a bit away from a campfire because you got too warm, only to have the wind hit it and blow hot air over you? Same thought exercise applies here.

sentences.en: "there's no such thing as a closed loop system"That's not incorrect though; you're still needing to shed the heat energy SOMEWHERE.
sentences.zh: “不存在闭环系统这样的东西”但这并不是不正确的；你仍然需要在某个地方散发热能。

sentences.en: Thermodynamics alone dictates that basic fact.
sentences.zh: 热力学本身就决定了这个基本事实。

sentences.en: You may not waste the cooling solution but all that excessive GPU heat is going to need to go somewhere - and the only place it ultimately goes is right into the environment.Ever stand a bit away from a campfire because you got too warm, only to have the wind hit it and blow hot air over you?
sentences.zh: 您可能不会浪费冷却解决方案，但所有多余的 GPU 热量都需要转移到某个地方 - 而它最终到达的唯一地方就是直接进入环境。您是否曾经因为天气太热而站在离篝火稍远的地方，结果却被风吹到上面，将热空气吹到您身上？

sentences.en: Same thought exercise applies here.
sentences.zh: 同样的思维练习也适用于此。

blocks.html_en:
Except that's not what the article is about.

What it's about is this:

This one data center in Ohio claimed to be a closed loop system, but then dumped their blowdown directly into the water table or something and now everyone's tap water is weird.

That is miles away from how your average "closed loop" system operates. There should be accountability for DC operators, but that's not what people take away from the article. What people take away from the article is, "All the data center companies lie, and are the worst sorts of damn dirty polluters and they will ruin your town".

sentences.en: Except that's not what the article is about.What it's about is this:This one data center in Ohio claimed to be a closed loop system, but then dumped their blowdown directly into the water table or something and now everyone's tap water is weird.That is miles away from how your average "closed loop" system operates.
sentences.zh: 但这不是本文的主题。它的主题是这样的：俄亥俄州的这个数据中心声称是一个闭环系统，但随后将排污直接倾倒到地下水位或其他地方，现在每个人的自来水都很奇怪。这与普通“闭环”系统的运行方式相去甚远。

sentences.en: There should be accountability for DC operators, but that's not what people take away from the article.
sentences.zh: 数据中心运营商应该承担责任，但这并不是人们从这篇文章中得到的结论。

sentences.en: What people take away from the article is, "All the data center companies lie, and are the worst sorts of damn dirty polluters and they will ruin your town".
sentences.zh: 人们从这篇文章中得到的结论是，“所有数据中心公司都在撒谎，而且是最糟糕的肮脏污染者，他们会毁掉你的城镇”。

blocks.html_en:
You're putting words in my mouth. Don't.

The government has the kind of power needed to rein in all of these privacy issues by regulating big tech. It will be difficult, but more difficult things have been achieved in our lifetime. Join the EFF. Contact your congress people.

What I'm actually saying is the anti-DC people are trying to solve a societal level problem only peripherally related to the thing they are trying to ban at a local level. That seems a lot harder than getting congress to care about privacy issues.

sentences.en: You're putting words in my mouth.
sentences.zh: 你把话放到我嘴里。

sentences.en: Don't.The government has the kind of power needed to rein in all of these privacy issues by regulating big tech.
sentences.zh: 不要。政府拥有通过监管大型科技来控制所有这些隐私问题所需的权力。

sentences.en: It will be difficult, but more difficult things have been achieved in our lifetime.
sentences.zh: 这会很困难，但在我们的一生中已经实现了更困难的事情。

sentences.en: Join the EFF.
sentences.zh: 加入 EFF。

sentences.en: Contact your congress people.What I'm actually saying is the anti-DC people are trying to solve a societal level problem only peripherally related to the thing they are trying to ban at a local level.
sentences.zh: 联系你的国会议员。我实际上想说的是，反华盛顿人士正在试图解决一个与他们试图在地方层面禁止的事物无关的社会层面问题。

sentences.en: That seems a lot harder than getting congress to care about privacy issues.
sentences.zh: 这似乎比让国会关心隐私问题要困难得多。

blocks.html_en:
> those that want data centers are doing their damndest to somehow claim that they’re not responsible for the rise in the cost of utilities and shouldn’t be held responsible for it

Because they're not. That's a utility billing issue. The power company can charge residential customers lower rates than it does commercial/industrial customers. If it's not doing that, then you're better off taking it up with them (or quite possibly: whichever regulatory body is hindering their ability to do that).

sentences.en: > those that want data centers are doing their damndest to somehow claim that they’re not responsible for the rise in the cost of utilities and shouldn’t be held responsible for itBecause they're not.
sentences.zh: > 那些想要数据中心的人正在尽最大努力以某种方式声称他们对公用事业成本的上涨不负有责任，也不应该对此负责，因为他们没有。

sentences.en: That's a utility billing issue.
sentences.zh: 这是一个公用事业计费问题。

sentences.en: The power company can charge residential customers lower rates than it does commercial/industrial customers.
sentences.zh: 电力公司可以向住宅客户收取比商业/工业客户更低的费率。

sentences.en: If it's not doing that, then you're better off taking it up with them (or quite possibly: whichever regulatory body is hindering their ability to do that).
sentences.zh: 如果它没有这样做，那么你最好向他们提出这个问题（或者很可能：无论哪个监管机构阻碍了他们这样做的能力）。

blocks.html_en:
> Folks care because their bottom line is impacted in a time when it’s already hard to make ends meet every month due to rising costs of food, gas, and utilities, not because of some unnamed boogeyman in the form of a “nation state competitor”

While agreeing with where you're coming from, including this, it is also worth nothing that there is truth to what you're rejecting as well. Our longstanding unsolved domestic problems are fertile ground for nation state competitors to foment discontent, gain oppositional mindshare, and ultimately harm our overall position. The elites seem to think these problems can just be ignored or swept under the rug, when in reality the frustration grows until it boils over into highly destructive self-owns. We're already seeing this actively play out on a grand scale with general politics, but it can certainly happen in more specific ways that target things like data centers.

To be industrially competitive we need to fix our longstanding domestic problems that fall on individuals, rather than merely ignoring them. In the case of data centers - lack of effective environmental regulations around air and sound pollution, complete inability for municipalities to regulate the operations of something after it's been built, and a shortage of infrastructure that's now been privatized for use by the highest bidder.

Plus that general affordability crisis you touched upon, of course. Nobody likes the feeling that an appendage of a trillion dollar company can come to their city, wave around the promise of everything-bubble money, and make it so their own individual voice is meaningless.

sentences.en: > Folks care because their bottom line is impacted in a time when it’s already hard to make ends meet every month due to rising costs of food, gas, and utilities, not because of some unnamed boogeyman in the form of a “nation state competitor”While agreeing with where you're coming from, including this, it is also worth nothing that there is truth to what you're rejecting as well.
sentences.zh: > 人们之所以关心，是因为他们的底线受到影响，因为食品、天然气和公用事业成本的上涨，每个月已经很难维持收支平衡，而不是因为某个“民族国家竞争对手”形式的无名恶人。虽然同意你的观点，包括这一点，但你所拒绝的东西也是事实，这也是毫无价值的。

sentences.en: Our longstanding unsolved domestic problems are fertile ground for nation state competitors to foment discontent, gain oppositional mindshare, and ultimately harm our overall position.
sentences.zh: 我们长期未解决的国内问题是民族国家竞争对手煽动不满、赢得反对派思想并最终损害我们整体地位的沃土。

sentences.en: The elites seem to think these problems can just be ignored or swept under the rug, when in reality the frustration grows until it boils over into highly destructive self-owns.
sentences.zh: 精英们似乎认为这些问题可以被忽视或掩盖起来，而实际上，挫败感不断增加，直到沸腾成极具破坏性的自我拥有。

sentences.en: We're already seeing this actively play out on a grand scale with general politics, but it can certainly happen in more specific ways that target things like data centers.To be industrially competitive we need to fix our longstanding domestic problems that fall on individuals, rather than merely ignoring them.
sentences.zh: 我们已经看到这种情况在一般政治中大规模地积极发挥作用，但它肯定可以以更具体的方式发生，针对数据中心等目标。为了保持产业竞争力，我们需要解决长期存在的个人问题，而​​不是仅仅忽视它们。

sentences.en: In the case of data centers - lack of effective environmental regulations around air and sound pollution, complete inability for municipalities to regulate the operations of something after it's been built, and a shortage of infrastructure that's now been privatized for use by the highest bidder.Plus that general affordability crisis you touched upon, of course.
sentences.zh: 就数据中心而言，缺乏针对空气和声音污染的有效环境法规，市政当局完全无力监管建成后的运营，以及基础设施短缺，而这些基础设施现已私有化供最高出价者使用。当然，还有您提到的普遍负担能力危机。

sentences.en: Nobody likes the feeling that an appendage of a trillion dollar company can come to their city, wave around the promise of everything-bubble money, and make it so their own individual voice is meaningless.
sentences.zh: 没有人喜欢这样的感觉：价值数万亿美元的公司的附属品可以来到他们的城市，挥舞着一切的承诺——泡沫货币，并使他们自己的个人声音变得毫无意义。

blocks.html_en:
> Folks care because their bottom line is impacted in a time when it’s already hard to make ends meet every month due to rising costs of food, gas, and utilities, not because of some unnamed boogeyman in the form of a “nation state competitor”

While agreeing with where you're coming from, including this, it is also worth nothing that there is truth to what you're rejecting as well. Our longstanding unsolved domestic problems are fertile ground for nation state competitors to foment discontent, gain oppositional mindshare, and ultimately harm our overall position. The elites seem to think these problems can just be ignored or swept under the rug, when in reality the frustration grows until it boils over into highly destructive self-owns. We're already seeing this actively play out on a grand scale with general politics, but it can certainly happen in more specific ways that target things like data centers.

To be industrially competitive we need to fix our longstanding domestic problems that fall on individuals, rather than merely ignoring them. In the case of data centers - lack of effective environmental regulations around air and sound pollution, complete inability for municipalities to regulate the operations of something after it's been built, and a shortage of infrastructure that's now been privatized for use by the highest bidder.

Plus that general affordability crisis you touched upon, of course. Nobody likes the feeling that an appendage of a trillion dollar company can come to their city, wave around the promise of everything-bubble money, and make it so their own individual voice is meaningless.

sentences.en: > Folks care because their bottom line is impacted in a time when it’s already hard to make ends meet every month due to rising costs of food, gas, and utilities, not because of some unnamed boogeyman in the form of a “nation state competitor”While agreeing with where you're coming from, including this, it is also worth nothing that there is truth to what you're rejecting as well.
sentences.zh: > 人们之所以关心，是因为他们的底线受到影响，因为食品、天然气和公用事业成本的上涨，每个月已经很难维持收支平衡，而不是因为某个“民族国家竞争对手”形式的无名恶人。虽然同意你的观点，包括这一点，但你所拒绝的东西也是事实，这也是毫无价值的。

sentences.en: Our longstanding unsolved domestic problems are fertile ground for nation state competitors to foment discontent, gain oppositional mindshare, and ultimately harm our overall position.
sentences.zh: 我们长期未解决的国内问题是民族国家竞争对手煽动不满、赢得反对派思想并最终损害我们整体地位的沃土。

blocks.html_en:
Blaming Russia (and China) in most cases is a cheap cop out to try to deflect, hide and minimize the actual grievances of people living in USA.

Yes, there's propaganda and most of it doesn't reach the Americans. It's downright insulting to claim their hurts are something that countries on the other side of the world invented.

sentences.en: Blaming Russia (and China) in most cases is a cheap cop out to try to deflect, hide and minimize the actual grievances of people living in USA.Yes, there's propaganda and most of it doesn't reach the Americans.
sentences.zh: 在大多数情况下，指责俄罗斯（和中国）是一种廉价的借口，试图转移、隐藏和最小化生活在美国的人们的实际不满。是的，有宣传，但其中大部分都没有到达美国人的手中。

sentences.en: It's downright insulting to claim their hurts are something that countries on the other side of the world invented.
sentences.zh: 声称他们的伤害是世界另一端的国家发明的，这是彻头彻尾的侮辱。

blocks.html_en:
Not what is being claimed here and stating that shows a complete lack of understanding of the term Active Measures, the history of it, and what the book of the same name discusses.

Also, it's amusing you think you know my political leanings or that it would somehow affect the accuracy of what is being discussed. It's strange how people think that they can win arguments by casting the person they're debating into some group they've decided is already wrong.

Anyways, go google the term and then pretend like you knew what it was all along and that you're an expert on Russian influence campaigns of the 20th and 21st centuries. That works well on Twitter and Reddit, maybe it'll work here too?

blocks.html_en:
> They’re not responsible for the rise in the cost of utilities and shouldn’t be held responsible for it.

One thing that has near radicalized me is the rampant dishonesty on this subject. Data center builders/users have the audacity to tell me that my rates aren't going up, but also if they are it isn't their fault, but even if it is their fault they will actually lower my rates in the long term (but somehow, never promising to pay us back for the damage in the short term).

It really, really bothers me how slimy the people doing this are.

sentences.en: > They’re not responsible for the rise in the cost of utilities and shouldn’t be held responsible for it.One thing that has near radicalized me is the rampant dishonesty on this subject.
sentences.zh: > 他们对公用事业成本的上涨不负有责任，也不应该为此承担责任。让我近乎激进的一件事是在这个问题上猖獗的不诚实行为。

sentences.en: Data center builders/users have the audacity to tell me that my rates aren't going up, but also if they are it isn't their fault, but even if it is their fault they will actually lower my rates in the long term (but somehow, never promising to pay us back for the damage in the short term).It really, really bothers me how slimy the people doing this are.
sentences.zh: 数据中心建设者/用户竟然厚颜无耻地告诉我，我的费率没有上涨，但即使是他们的错，也不是他们的错，但即使是他们的错，从长远来看，他们实际上也会降低我的费率（但不知何故，他们从不承诺在短期内赔偿我们的损失）。这真的让我很困扰，做这件事的人是多么的狡猾。

blocks.html_en:
> No one ever cared about DCs before now.

This isn't true. People were starting to mobilize against crypto datacenters in the late 2010s and early 2020s^[1][2], long before the current AI-fueled building frenzy.

> I think there’s a lot of social media influence from nation state competitors going on to try and slow SOTA development in the US.

It's fairly well documented that this is happening to some degree, but as above, foreign agents didn't conjure the anti-datacenter movement out of nothing. It's a fact that if the average crypto or AI datacenter moves in next door to you, there's a good chance it's going to crash your quality of life and the value of your property, and all in service of making a few fantastically rich guys even richer. Also, they are the same guys building a machine that they claim is going to destroy your livelihood. You don't need foreign agents to engineer a backlash here.

[1] https://e360.yale.edu/features/bitcoins-intensive-energy-dem... [2] https://www.texastribune.org/2025/10/09/texas-hood-county-cr...

sentences.en: > No one ever cared about DCs before now.This isn't true.
sentences.zh: > 以前没有人关心过 DC。这不是真的。

sentences.en: People were starting to mobilize against crypto datacenters in the late 2010s and early 2020s^[1][2], long before the current AI-fueled building frenzy.> I think there’s a lot of social media influence from nation state competitors going on to try and slow SOTA development in the US.It's fairly well documented that this is happening to some degree, but as above, foreign agents didn't conjure the anti-datacenter movement out of nothing.
sentences.zh: 人们在 2010 年代末和 2020 年代初就开始动员起来反对加密数据中心^[1][2]，早在当前人工智能推动的建筑狂潮之前。>我认为，民族国家竞争对手在社交媒体上产生了很大的影响力，试图减缓美国 SOTA 的发展。有充分的证据表明这种情况在某种程度上正在发生，但如上所述，外国特工并没有凭空变出反数据中心运动。

sentences.en: It's a fact that if the average crypto or AI datacenter moves in next door to you, there's a good chance it's going to crash your quality of life and the value of your property, and all in service of making a few fantastically rich guys even richer.
sentences.zh: 事实上，如果普通的加密或人工智能数据中心搬到你的隔壁，它很有可能会降低你的生活质量和你的财产价值，而这一切都是为了让一些超级富有的人变得更加富有。

sentences.en: Also, they are the same guys building a machine that they claim is going to destroy your livelihood.
sentences.zh: 而且，他们也是制造一台机器的人，他们声称这台机器会毁掉你的生计。

sentences.en: You don't need foreign agents to engineer a backlash here.[1] https://e360.yale.edu/features/bitcoins-intensive-energy-dem... [2] https://www.texastribune.org/2025/10/09/texas-hood-county-cr...
sentences.zh: 你不需要外国特工在这里引发强烈反对。[1] https://e360.yale.edu/features/bitcoins-intense-energy-dem... [2] https://www.texastribune.org/2025/10/09/texas-hood-county-cr...

blocks.html_en:
My country banned all hyperscalers. Has nothing to do with China- I live in a densely populated country that doesn't need the chump change from Google and Microsoft.

Ugly giant buildings that take up valuable land and require lots of infrastructure meanwhile providing no jobs are a very bad deal.

sentences.en: My country banned all hyperscalers.
sentences.zh: 我国禁止所有超大规模企业。

sentences.en: Has nothing to do with China- I live in a densely populated country that doesn't need the chump change from Google and Microsoft.Ugly giant buildings that take up valuable land and require lots of infrastructure meanwhile providing no jobs are a very bad deal.
sentences.zh: 与中国无关——我生活在一个人口稠密的国家，不需要谷歌和微软的小钱。丑陋的巨型建筑占用了宝贵的土地，需要大量的基础设施，同时又不提供就业机会，这是一笔非常糟糕的交易。

blocks.html_en:
Yeah. IMO anyone arguing (explicitly or otherwise) that these facilities don't come with enormous negative externalities for people unlucky enough to live near them is either ignorant or being disingenuous. There is no need to lean on contentious minutiae like the water usage thing to show that this is true.

If AI development has such strategic urgency and importance that this desperate rapid expansion at any cost is reasonably justified on national security grounds, the government should be coordinating access to land and power and making people whole when their communities are impacted. Instead, you see unlucky everyday Americans left holding the bag while datacenter operators trick and bribe their way into building these projects and then run roughshod over regulations (e.g. how many open cycle gas turbines are you permitted to have running in your parking lot, mr. Musk?) because there is nobody willing to enforce them.

Honestly, any attempt to dismiss this all as groundless NIMBYism is unbelievably disgusting and makes me think that China and Russia are not the only parties running influence operations here. I do indeed see a strong push to frame the anti-datacenter position with weak/unsupported points (e.g. water usage) rather than strong and supported points (noise, electricity use, carbon footprint).

sentences.en: Yeah.
sentences.zh: 是的。

sentences.en: IMO anyone arguing (explicitly or otherwise) that these facilities don't come with enormous negative externalities for people unlucky enough to live near them is either ignorant or being disingenuous.
sentences.zh: 在我看来，如果有人（明确或以其他方式）认为这些设施不会给那些不幸居住在它们附近的人们带来巨大的负外部性，那么他要么是无知，要么是不诚实。

sentences.en: There is no need to lean on contentious minutiae like the water usage thing to show that this is true.If AI development has such strategic urgency and importance that this desperate rapid expansion at any cost is reasonably justified on national security grounds, the government should be coordinating access to land and power and making people whole when their communities are impacted.
sentences.zh: 没有必要依靠像用水问题这样有争议的细节来证明这是真的。如果人工智能的发展具有如此的战略紧迫性和重要性，以至于这种不惜一切代价的绝望快速扩张在国家安全方面是合理的，那么政府应该协调土地和电力的获取，并在社区受到影响时让人们保持完整。

sentences.en: Instead, you see unlucky everyday Americans left holding the bag while datacenter operators trick and bribe their way into building these projects and then run roughshod over regulations (e.g. how many open cycle gas turbines are you permitted to have running in your parking lot, mr.
sentences.zh: 相反，你会看到不幸的美国人每天都背负着包袱，而数据中心运营商通过欺骗和贿赂来建设这些项目，然后粗暴地违反法规（例如，你允许在停车场运行多少台开式循环燃气轮机，先生。

sentences.en: Musk?)
sentences.zh: 马斯克？）

sentences.en: because there is nobody willing to enforce them.Honestly, any attempt to dismiss this all as groundless NIMBYism is unbelievably disgusting and makes me think that China and Russia are not the only parties running influence operations here.
sentences.zh: 因为没有人愿意执行这些规定。老实说，任何将这一切视为毫无根据的邻避主义的企图都是令人难以置信的恶心，让我认为中国和俄罗斯并不是唯一在这里开展影响力行动的各方。

sentences.en: I do indeed see a strong push to frame the anti-datacenter position with weak/unsupported points (e.g. water usage) rather than strong and supported points (noise, electricity use, carbon footprint).
sentences.zh: 我确实看到人们强烈推动用薄弱/不受支持的点（例如水的使用）而不是强的和受支持的点（噪音、电力使用、碳足迹）来构建反数据中心的立场。

sentences.zh: 因为没有人愿意执行这些规定。老实说，任何将这一切视为毫无根据的邻避主义的企图都是令人难以置信的恶心，让我认为中国和俄罗斯并不是唯一在这里开展影响力行动的各方。

sentences.en: I do indeed see a strong push to frame the anti-datacenter position with weak/unsupported points (e.g. water usage) rather than strong and supported points (noise, electricity use, carbon footprint).
sentences.zh: 我确实看到人们强烈推动用薄弱/不受支持的点（例如水的使用）而不是强的和受支持的点（噪音、电力使用、碳足迹）来构建反数据中心的立场。

blocks.html_en: Yeah. IMO anyone arguing (explicitly or otherwise) that these facilities don't come with enormous negative externalities for people unlucky enough to live near them is either ignorant or being disingenuous. There is no need to lean on contentious minutiae like the water usage thing to show that this is true.

sentences.en: Yeah.
sentences.zh: 是的。

sentences.en: IMO anyone arguing (explicitly or otherwise) that these facilities don't come with enormous negative externalities for people unlucky enough to live near them is either ignorant or being disingenuous.
sentences.zh: 在我看来，如果有人（明确或以其他方式）认为这些设施不会给那些不幸居住在它们附近的人们带来巨大的负外部性，那么他要么是无知，要么是不诚实。

sentences.en: There is no need to lean on contentious minutiae like the water usage thing to show that this is true.
sentences.zh: 没有必要依靠像用水量这样有争议的细节来证明这是真的。

blocks.html_en: If AI development has such strategic urgency and importance that this desperate rapid expansion at any cost is reasonably justified on national security grounds, the government should be coordinating access to land and power and making people whole when their communities are impacted. Instead, you see unlucky everyday Americans left holding the bag while datacenter operators trick and bribe their way into building these projects and then run roughshod over regulations (e.g. how many open cycle gas turbines are you permitted to have running in your parking lot, mr. Musk?) because there is nobody willing to enforce them.

sentences.zh: 如果人工智能发展具有如此的战略紧迫性和重要性，以至于这种不惜一切代价的绝望快速扩张在国家安全方面是合理的，那么政府应该协调土地和电力的获取，并在社区受到影响时让人们得到完整的保障。

blocks.html_en:
People seem pretty sour on big tech in general, maybe it's because, I don't know...

Data centers being directly tied to increases in our power bills.

Or CEOs talking about how they're going to get to fire everyone because of AI.

Or social media raising the political temperature.

Or all of them combined. Tech has never been so intolerable across the board.

I think its a proxy for general populist sentiment.

"These AI people are getting rich selling unicorn farts while I'm on the same pay and everything is more expensive, fuck them".

blocks.html_en:
It’s more than one thing. Yes there’s definitely some populism and maybe some astroturfing from outside groups - but actual electricity costs are going up and the average consumer can’t really get anymore efficient, so they’re indirectly subsidizing this stuff. Computers and related hardware have gone up in price. And then you’ve got all of the “AI layoffs” that also result in real-world pain.

Ignore at your own peril.

sentences.en: It’s more than one thing.
sentences.zh: 这不仅仅是一件事。

sentences.en: Yes there’s definitely some populism and maybe some astroturfing from outside groups - but actual electricity costs are going up and the average consumer can’t really get anymore efficient, so they’re indirectly subsidizing this stuff.
sentences.zh: 是的，肯定有一些民粹主义，也许还有一些来自外部团体的“草皮”——但实际的电力成本正在上升，普通消费者无法真正获得更高的效率，所以他们间接补贴了这些东西。

sentences.en: Computers and related hardware have gone up in price.
sentences.zh: 计算机和相关硬件的价格上涨。

sentences.en: And then you’ve got all of the “AI layoffs” that also result in real-world pain.Ignore at your own peril.
sentences.zh: 然后你就会遇到所有的“人工智能裁员”，这也会导致现实世界的痛苦。忽视后果自负。

blocks.html_en: DCs have a tendency to destabilize the local grid and drive up grid rates and have ridiculous water consumption requirements, both largely externalized to the surrounding community, so it's more a matter of basic self-defense than any notional populist agenda.

blocks.html_en:
> No one ever cared about DCs before now.

Just because you were not looking doesn't mean it wasn't happening. Anti-DC sentiment has been high in some places before LLMs came on to the scene. Even HN has had multiple submissions of towns in rural areas fighting not to let Meta (or similar companies) build datacenters in their town.

(On the flip side, I know at least one town that welcomed them!)

blocks.html_en:
> No one ever cared about DCs before now.

Just because you were not looking doesn't mean it wasn't happening. Anti-DC sentiment has been high in some places before LLMs came on to the scene. Even HN has had multiple submissions of towns in rural areas fighting not to let Meta (or similar companies) build datacenters in their town.

(On the flip side, I know at least one town that welcomed them!)
