blocks.html_en:
> No one ever cared about DCs before now.

Society hasn't felt the very real effects of data centers until recently. Power supply being short meant that residential customers have had to pick up the tab for heavy industrial users. We've started to learn just how bad all those dams we built are for the environment and many of them are tied to data center money. Elon Musk waltzed into Memphis and stood up a natural gas data center that has basically poisoned the surrounding area.

Maybe nation state actors are helping, that can always be true. It is also true that the American people are starting to realize they've been had many times over and are just starting to ask for a better deal. I think we should listen.

sentences.en: > No one ever cared about DCs before now.Society hasn't felt the very real effects of data centers until recently.
sentences.zh: > 以前没有人关心数据中心。直到最近，社会才感受到数据中心的真正影响。

sentences.en: Power supply being short meant that residential customers have had to pick up the tab for heavy industrial users.
sentences.zh: 电力供应短缺意味着住宅用户必须为重工业用户买单。

sentences.en: We've started to learn just how bad all those dams we built are for the environment and many of them are tied to data center money.
sentences.zh: 我们已经开始了解我们建造的所有水坝对环境有多么糟糕，其中许多水坝都与数据中心的资金挂钩。

sentences.en: Elon Musk waltzed into Memphis and stood up a natural gas data center that has basically poisoned the surrounding area.Maybe nation state actors are helping, that can always be true.
sentences.zh: 埃隆·马斯克 (Elon Musk) 走进孟菲斯，建立了一个天然气数据中心，基本上毒害了周边地区。也许国家行为者正在提供帮助，这永远是事实。

sentences.en: It is also true that the American people are starting to realize they've been had many times over and are just starting to ask for a better deal.
sentences.zh: 确实，美国人民开始意识到他们已经遭受过多次打击，并开始要求达成更好的协议。

sentences.en: I think we should listen.
sentences.zh: 我想我们应该听听。

blocks.html_en:
> Power supply being short meant that residential customers have had to pick up the tab for heavy industrial users.

Maybe some kind of progressive pricing for power would be appropriate. I'm not sure if this exists, and correct me if it does, but maybe the second MW should cost an order of magnitude more then the first, and so on.

blocks.html_en:
If only we had a limitless form of energy that comes out of the sky. Or if that doesn't work, maybe there's some miraculous form of energy that can be extracted from rocks, produces no emissions whatsoever, and could power society for thousands of years.

Nah, let's make a really convoluted system to jack up energy prices for everyone, and increasingly rely on a dwindling supply of non-renewable energy.

sentences.en: If only we had a limitless form of energy that comes out of the sky.
sentences.zh: 要是我们有一种来自天空的无限能量就好了。

sentences.en: Or if that doesn't work, maybe there's some miraculous form of energy that can be extracted from rocks, produces no emissions whatsoever, and could power society for thousands of years.Nah, let's make a really convoluted system to jack up energy prices for everyone, and increasingly rely on a dwindling supply of non-renewable energy.
sentences.zh: 或者如果这不起作用，也许有某种神奇的能源可以从岩石中提取，不产生任何排放，并且可以为社会提供数千年的电力。不，让我们建立一个非常复杂的系统来抬高每个人的能源价格，并越来越依赖日益减少的不可再生能源供应。

blocks.html_en:
The data center construction boom has precipitated a lot of inflation in electronics and related fields, because the data centers are buying out all of the silicon manufacturing and RAM manufacturing and etc, and all of this stuff is horribly supply-limited. And the people pushing for data center construction are proudly telling us that we need to support this so that we can put half of the population out of work, oh, and we just need a small trillion-dollar subsidy or five to pay for it all (because demand for AI isn't high enough to pay for all of it themselves).

There's no need to invent some malign foreign conspirator to generate unpopularity for this stuff. No, the AI companies are led by unpopular CEOs, who are making unpopular statements (almost cartoonishly so). And opposing a data center is the kind of action that regular people, upset about this sort of stuff, feel that they can actually meaningfully effect. So of course it was going to balloon like it has.

sentences.en: The data center construction boom has precipitated a lot of inflation in electronics and related fields, because the data centers are buying out all of the silicon manufacturing and RAM manufacturing and etc, and all of this stuff is horribly supply-limited.
sentences.zh: 数据中心建设热潮引发了电子及相关领域的大量通货膨胀，因为数据中心正在买断所有的硅制造和RAM制造等，而所有这些东西的供应都严重有限。

sentences.en: And the people pushing for data center construction are proudly telling us that we need to support this so that we can put half of the population out of work, oh, and we just need a small trillion-dollar subsidy or five to pay for it all (because demand for AI isn't high enough to pay for all of it themselves).There's no need to invent some malign foreign conspirator to generate unpopularity for this stuff.
sentences.zh: 推动数据中心建设的人们自豪地告诉我们，我们需要支持这一点，这样我们就可以让一半的人口失业，哦，我们只需要一小笔一万亿美元或五美元的补贴来支付这一切（因为对人工智能的需求还不足以自己支付所有费用）。没有必要发明一些恶意的外国阴谋家来让这些东西不受欢迎。

sentences.en: No, the AI companies are led by unpopular CEOs, who are making unpopular statements (almost cartoonishly so).
sentences.zh: 不，人工智能公司是由不受欢迎的首席执行官领导的，他们发表了不受欢迎的声明（几乎是卡通化的）。

sentences.en: And opposing a data center is the kind of action that regular people, upset about this sort of stuff, feel that they can actually meaningfully effect.
sentences.zh: 反对数据中心是普通人对此类事情感到不安的行动，他们认为他们实际上可以产生有意义的影响。

sentences.en: So of course it was going to balloon like it has.
sentences.zh: 所以它当然会像现在这样膨胀。

blocks.html_en:
>No one ever cared about DCs before now.

Fewer people cared, sure.. but it's because until recently they weren't eclipsing power consumption of extremely energy-heavy industries, noticeably driving local residential energy prices up.

They also weren't so heavily noise polluting nor were they typically built in places where their noise pollution affected so many people.

blocks.html_en:
where does the noise pollution come from? GPU fans? What's more noisy about a DC filled with CPUs and GPUs than a DC filled with just CPUs. I can imagine generators being noisy but that wouldn't be anything different than regular data centers. Also, so few DCs for the AI buildout are actually online where are the noise measurements taken from?

I understand if people don't want a DC near them i just don't understand why they care now. I have some pretty big ones about 3miles from me and next door to both are residential areas and businesses. In fact, some of the biggest ones where i live (Dallas) are so understated you have to know it's a datacenter beforehand to even realize what you're looking at. There are some really large ones in Carrolton TX that are nestled right up to neighborhoods, i bet 95% of the people don't even know they're there and the 5% that do probably work there or host there.

the carrolton dc https://maps.app.goo.gl/mv3wo96ZTuhRBkGy5

blocks.html_en:
The difference in noise pollution comes from power density & power generation, if they're doing so on site. The power demand (and resulting waste heat) absolutely eclipses that of data centers of olde.

If they're not generating power on site & creating noise/consuming local natural gas resources, they're guzzling grid power and almost certainly contributing to electrical rate increases that affect residential customers disproportionately.

sentences.en: The difference in noise pollution comes from power density & power generation, if they're doing so on site.
sentences.zh: 噪音污染的差异来自于功率密度和发电量（如果是在现场的话）。

sentences.en: The power demand (and resulting waste heat) absolutely eclipses that of data centers of olde.If they're not generating power on site & creating noise/consuming local natural gas resources, they're guzzling grid power and almost certainly contributing to electrical rate increases that affect residential customers disproportionately.
sentences.zh: 电力需求（以及由此产生的废热）绝对使旧数据中心黯然失色。如果它们不现场发电并产生噪音/消耗当地天然气资源，它们就会消耗电网电力，并且几乎肯定会导致电价上涨，从而对住宅客户产生不成比例的影响。

blocks.html_en:
I made this comment a little bit ago, I'll share it again:

I think the biggest change in public sentiment in datacenters is a reaction to the extreme scale they're being deployed at these days. They're really vastly unlike datacenters of the past. The power densities are so much higher, they're so much larger, and they're getting vastly more environmental waivers and tax subsidies than ever before.

A quiet, average-sized warehouse-like building in an industrial area with a big power substation next to it often didn't bother a lot of people. A giant monster of a building being built in what was previously people's backyards with howling on-site power generation that got installed without any kind of air quality reviews because they're allegedly "temporary" and starts sucking the groudwater dry is a whole different story.

This is a datacenter: https://maps.app.goo.gl/pzSsUHXPaUuLGaDa9 I doubt many people even know its a datacenter. Its pretty much any other office building.

This is also a datacenter. I imagine just about everyone living nearby knows its a datacenter. https://maps.app.goo.gl/wR4NgcWQVhJAy5Gy6

Now, don't get me wrong, I do agree there's a lot of social media frenzy in reaction to all datacenters. There are a lot of datacenters around me, and it seems like roughly nobody cared when they were built. They've been there for years, some have been operational for decades. Suddenly now, lots of people have a problem with all of them.

sentences.en: I made this comment a little bit ago, I'll share it again:I think the biggest change in public sentiment in datacenters is a reaction to the extreme scale they're being deployed at these days.
sentences.zh: 我不久前发表过这个评论，我将再次分享：我认为数据中心公众情绪的最大变化是对这些天部署的极端规模的反应。

sentences.en: They're really vastly unlike datacenters of the past.
sentences.zh: 它们确实与过去的数据中心截然不同。

sentences.en: The power densities are so much higher, they're so much larger, and they're getting vastly more environmental waivers and tax subsidies than ever before.A quiet, average-sized warehouse-like building in an industrial area with a big power substation next to it often didn't bother a lot of people.
sentences.zh: 功率密度高得多，体积大得多，而且获得的环境豁免和税收补贴比以往任何时候都多得多。工业区中一栋安静的、中等大小的仓库式建筑，旁边有一个大型变电站，通常不会打扰很多人。

sentences.en: A giant monster of a building being built in what was previously people's backyards with howling on-site power generation that got installed without any kind of air quality reviews because they're allegedly "temporary" and starts sucking the groudwater dry is a whole different story.This is a datacenter: https://maps.app.goo.gl/pzSsUHXPaUuLGaDa9 I doubt many people even know its a datacenter.
sentences.zh: 一座巨大的建筑建在以前人们的后院，现场发电，在没有任何空气质量审查的情况下安装，因为据称它们是“临时的”，并开始吸干地下水，这是一个完全不同的故事。这是一个数据中心：https://maps.app.goo.gl/pzSsUHXPaUuLGaDa9 我怀疑很多人甚至不知道它是一个数据中心。

sentences.en: Its pretty much any other office building.This is also a datacenter.
sentences.zh: 它几乎是任何其他办公楼。这也是一个数据中心。

sentences.en: I imagine just about everyone living nearby knows its a datacenter.
sentences.zh: 我想几乎所有住在附近的人都知道它是一个数据中心。

sentences.en: https://maps.app.goo.gl/wR4NgcWQVhJAy5Gy6Now, don't get me wrong, I do agree there's a lot of social media frenzy in reaction to all datacenters.
sentences.zh: https://maps.app.goo.gl/wR4NgcWQVhJAy5Gy6现在，不要误会我的意思，我确实同意所有数据中心都有很多社交媒体的狂热反应。

sentences.en: There are a lot of datacenters around me, and it seems like roughly nobody cared when they were built.
sentences.zh: 我周围有很多数据中心，但似乎在它们建成时几乎没有人关心。

sentences.en: They've been there for years, some have been operational for decades.
sentences.zh: 它们已经存在多年，有些已经运行了几十年。

sentences.en: Suddenly now, lots of people have a problem with all of them.
sentences.zh: 突然之间，很多人都遇到了问题。

blocks.html_en: A quiet, average-sized warehouse-like building in an industrial area with a big power substation next to it often didn't bother a lot of people. A giant monster of a building being built in what was previously people's backyards with howling on-site power generation that got installed without any kind of air quality reviews because they're allegedly "temporary" and starts sucking the groudwater dry is a whole different story.

blocks.html_en:
Your dad's data centers are very different to the new GPU focused beasts.

The AI data centers consume a lot more power, and thus generate a lot more heat(equals cooling noise) per square meter.

This is the reason why it's a new problem right now.

Edit: autocorrect used breasts instead of beasts.

sentences.en: Your dad's data centers are very different to the new GPU focused beasts.The AI data centers consume a lot more power, and thus generate a lot more heat(equals cooling noise) per square meter.This is the reason why it's a new problem right now.Edit: autocorrect used breasts instead of beasts.
sentences.zh: 你爸爸的数据中心与新的以 GPU 为中心的野兽有很大不同。人工智能数据中心消耗更多的电力，因此每平方米产生更多的热量（等于冷却噪音）。这就是为什么它现在是一个新问题的原因。编辑：自动更正使用的乳房而不是野兽。

blocks.html_en:
I hear and understand those, but why isn't it a regulation or requirements issue?

Ambient noise must be contained, excess heat must be mitigated, free hot water must be provided to nearby buildings. etc. Also this is a city's opportunity to do something like assess penalty tax points. (ie, datacenter usage tax, just like we do to hotels)

sentences.en: I hear and understand those, but why isn't it a regulation or requirements issue?Ambient noise must be contained, excess heat must be mitigated, free hot water must be provided to nearby buildings.
sentences.zh: 我听到并理解这些，但为什么这不是一个法规或要求问题？必须控制环境噪音，必须减轻多余的热量，必须向附近的建筑物提供免费热水。

sentences.en: etc. Also this is a city's opportunity to do something like assess penalty tax points.
sentences.zh: 等等。这也是一个城市进行诸如评估惩罚税点之类的事情的机会。

sentences.en: (ie, datacenter usage tax, just like we do to hotels)
sentences.zh: （即数据中心使用税，就像我们对酒店征收的税一样）

blocks.html_en:
> I hear and understand those, but why isn't it a regulation or requirements issue?

It's a waste of time and money for each town and county to come up with those requirements. If your community puts any limits on the datacenter builder they'll just build it somewhere else, so you might as well save the effort and just say they can't do it.

sentences.en: > I hear and understand those, but why isn't it a regulation or requirements issue?It's a waste of time and money for each town and county to come up with those requirements.
sentences.zh: > 我听到并理解这些，但为什么这不是一个法规或要求问题？每个镇和县提出这些要求都是浪费时间和金钱。

sentences.en: If your community puts any limits on the datacenter builder they'll just build it somewhere else, so you might as well save the effort and just say they can't do it.
sentences.zh: 如果您的社区对数据中心构建器有任何限制，他们只会在其他地方构建它，所以您不妨省点力气，直接说他们做不到。

blocks.html_en:
> I hear and understand those, but why isn't it a regulation or requirements issue?

It's a waste of time and money for each town and county to come up with those requirements. If your community puts any limits on the datacenter builder they'll just build it somewhere else, so you might as well save the effort and just say they can't do it.

sentences.en: If your community puts any limits on the datacenter builder they'll just build it somewhere else, so you might as well save the effort and just say they can't do it.
sentences.zh: 如果您的社区对数据中心构建器有任何限制，他们只会在其他地方构建它，所以您不妨省点力气，直接说他们做不到。

blocks.html_en:
the US is a republic of states. By design it wastes time and money to allow disagreement, variety, and self-determination of states.

> they'll just build it somewhere else

Why should we have rights to say they cannot do it somewhere else? It's not a moral issue, it's a tragedy of commons issue or unpriced externality issue.

sentences.en: the US is a republic of states.
sentences.zh: 美国是一个共和国。

sentences.en: By design it wastes time and money to allow disagreement, variety, and self-determination of states.> they'll just build it somewhere elseWhy should we have rights to say they cannot do it somewhere else?
sentences.zh: 从设计上来说，允许国家的分歧、多样性和自决是浪费时间和金钱的。>他们只会在其他地方建造它为什么我们有权利说他们不能在其他地方这样做？

sentences.en: It's not a moral issue, it's a tragedy of commons issue or unpriced externality issue.
sentences.zh: 这不是道德问题，而是公地悲剧或无价外部性问题。

blocks.html_en:
My point is the town has three options:

1) allow the build

2) develop a set of regulations to ensure the datacenter is a "good neighbor"

3) block the build

2 & 3 have the exact same effect

blocks.html_en:
>they'll just build it somewhere else

what seems to be happening in practice is that the companies building these data centers will just take you to court over the limits being illegal and make that fight cost more than the town's entire annual budget, thus inducing the town to give up.

sentences.en: >they'll just build it somewhere elsewhat seems to be happening in practice is that the companies building these data centers will just take you to court over the limits being illegal and make that fight cost more than the town's entire annual budget, thus inducing the town to give up.
sentences.zh: >他们只会在其他地方建造它实际上似乎发生的是，建造这些数据中心的公司只会将你告上法庭，因为这些限制是非法的，并使这场斗争的成本超过该镇的整个年度预算，从而促使该镇放弃。

blocks.html_en:
isn't that their freedom to? And the right/freedom of those who democratically created the low regulation environment?

I do not understand this idea that someone from one place should have their preferences taking precedence the preferences, rights and freedoms of folks who actually live in another place.

The main caveat being the boundary lines, but i think that's very much an edge case.

sentences.en: isn't that their freedom to?
sentences.zh: 这不是他们的自由吗？

sentences.en: And the right/freedom of those who democratically created the low regulation environment?I do not understand this idea that someone from one place should have their preferences taking precedence the preferences, rights and freedoms of folks who actually live in another place.The main caveat being the boundary lines, but i think that's very much an edge case.
sentences.zh: 那么那些以民主方式创造低监管环境的人的权利/自由呢？我不明白这种想法，即来自一个地方的人应该将自己的偏好优先于实际居住在另一个地方的人们的偏好、权利和自由。主要的警告是边界线，但我认为这在很大程度上是一种边缘情况。

blocks.html_en:
> but why isn't it a regulation or requirements issue?

It's America. They don't do regulations there.

The more serious answer is that this political movement is leading to more strict regulation being enacted, it just wasn't in place because of reason #1 and the problems being much less severe.

Additionally, the tech industry is notoriously hostile to any kind of regulation. Especially now with AI. Just look at how many people in this thread will insist that it's "Chinese Propaganda", despite clear evidence that this is just a response to the actual problems datacenters cause. One news crew interviewing people with jet turbine noise will do more than a million of Beijing's finest trolls.

sentences.en: > but why isn't it a regulation or requirements issue?It's America.
sentences.zh: > 但为什么这不是一个法规或要求问题？这是美国。

sentences.en: They don't do regulations there.The more serious answer is that this political movement is leading to more strict regulation being enacted, it just wasn't in place because of reason #1 and the problems being much less severe.Additionally, the tech industry is notoriously hostile to any kind of regulation.
sentences.zh: 他们在那里不进行监管。更严肃的答案是，这场政治运动正在导致制定更严格的监管，只是由于第一个原因而没有到位，而且问题没有那么严重。此外，众所周知，科技行业对任何形式的监管都怀有敌意。

sentences.en: Especially now with AI.
sentences.zh: 尤其是现在有了人工智能。

sentences.en: Just look at how many people in this thread will insist that it's "Chinese Propaganda", despite clear evidence that this is just a response to the actual problems datacenters cause.
sentences.zh: 看看这个帖子中有多少人会坚持认为这是“中国宣传”，尽管有明确的证据表明这只是对数据中心造成的实际问题的回应。

sentences.en: One news crew interviewing people with jet turbine noise will do more than a million of Beijing's finest trolls.
sentences.zh: 一个新闻工作人员采访带有喷气涡轮噪音的人将采访超过一百万北京最优秀的巨魔。

blocks.html_en:
Why is that a problem? It might raise the temp within a few hundred feet of the chillers by 0.5-1F which is substantially less than an urban heat island temp delta vs surrounding rural land.

A sane municipality will require sound mitigation on generators, chillers, and other loud equipment. Sure, there are shitty places that don’t take care of their citizens that will let you run natural gas turbines to generate power, but it’s possible to contain the bad effects of data centers with local and state regulations.

I’ll take a data center that evaporates water to replace the iron casting foundry in my neighborhood any day.

sentences.en: Why is that a problem?
sentences.zh: 为什么这是一个问题？

sentences.en: It might raise the temp within a few hundred feet of the chillers by 0.5-1F which is substantially less than an urban heat island temp delta vs surrounding rural land.A sane municipality will require sound mitigation on generators, chillers, and other loud equipment.
sentences.zh: 它可能会将冷水机组数百英尺内的温度提高 0.5-1F，这远低于城市热岛温度三角洲与周围农村土地的温度差。一个理智的城市将需要对发电机、冷水机组和其他噪音设备进行声音缓解。

sentences.en: Sure, there are shitty places that don’t take care of their citizens that will let you run natural gas turbines to generate power, but it’s possible to contain the bad effects of data centers with local and state regulations.I’ll take a data center that evaporates water to replace the iron casting foundry in my neighborhood any day.
sentences.zh: 当然，有一些不关心公民的糟糕地方会让你运行天然气涡轮机来发电，但通过地方和州法规来遏制数据中心的不良影响是可能的。我随时都会用一个可以蒸发水的数据中心来取代我附近的铸铁铸造厂。

sentences.en: Why is that a problem?
sentences.zh: 为什么这是一个问题？

sentences.en: It might raise the temp within a few hundred feet of the chillers by 0.5-1F which is substantially less than an urban heat island temp delta vs surrounding rural land.
sentences.zh: 它可能会将冷水机组几百英尺内的温度提高 0.5-1 华氏度，这远低于城市热岛温度三角洲与周围农村土地的温度差。

blocks.html_en: A sane municipality will require sound mitigation on generators, chillers, and other loud equipment. Sure, there are shitty places that don’t take care of their citizens that will let you run natural gas turbines to generate power, but it’s possible to contain the bad effects of data centers with local and state regulations.

sentences.en: A sane municipality will require sound mitigation on generators, chillers, and other loud equipment.
sentences.zh: 一个理智的城市需要对发电机、冷却器和其他噪音较大的设备进行降噪。

sentences.en: Sure, there are shitty places that don’t take care of their citizens that will let you run natural gas turbines to generate power, but it’s possible to contain the bad effects of data centers with local and state regulations.
sentences.zh: 当然，有些地方不关心他们的公民，可以让你运行天然气涡轮机发电，但可以通过地方和州法规来遏制数据中心的不良影响。

blocks.html_en: I’ll take a data center that evaporates water to replace the iron casting foundry in my neighborhood any day.

sentences.en: I’ll take a data center that evaporates water to replace the iron casting foundry in my neighborhood any day.
sentences.zh: 我随时都会用一个可以蒸发水的数据中心来取代我家附近的铸铁厂。

blocks.html_en:
It's not about the heat radiating from the data center, it's about the noise generated trying to cool the beast.

The power usage is massive, and that's still a very much limited resource for which everyone competes for.

Adding to your edit: Would you feel the same about adding an AI data center on top of the iron casting foundry you already have to deal with?

blocks.html_en:
You can buy chillers with enclosures that limit the sound to a reasonable volume, Google “chiller noise attenuation”. You can add walls around lots of chillers to further limit the noise.

If the data center follows the city statutes for noise levels at the property line that exist in my municipality, then sure, I’ll take the data center above the iron foundry.

> Power usage is massive

The utility can approve or deny the connection. Data centers in areas without any available power are not getting grid connections.

sentences.en: You can buy chillers with enclosures that limit the sound to a reasonable volume, Google “chiller noise attenuation”.
sentences.zh: 您可以购买带有外壳的冷水机，将声音限制在合理的音量，谷歌“冷水机噪音衰减”。

blocks.html_en: Your edit really illustrates what's wrong with all of this. We can't even get computers to reasonably assume what we're trying to say. But somehow the ultra rich have decided to devote all global resources to doing bad autocorrect for everything everywhere and calling it AI.

They probably would be awful to have a house next to, but which if the hyperscale data center proposals are near residential areas?

The really large data centers have to go into areas with a lot of land and utility connections. These are usually zoned commercial or industrial already.

sentences.en: They probably would be awful to have a house next to, but which if the hyperscale data center proposals are near residential areas?The really large data centers have to go into areas with a lot of land and utility connections.
sentences.zh: 旁边有一座房子可能会很糟糕，但如果超大规模数据中心提案位于住宅区附近，情况又会怎样呢？真正的大型数据中心必须进入拥有大量土地和公用设施连接的地区。

sentences.en: These are usually zoned commercial or industrial already.
sentences.zh: 这些通常已经被划为商业或工业区。

blocks.html_en:
We built data centers for decades without issue.

One I used to service frequently is actually surrounded by a large area of green-space, trees, marsh area, wildlife. The houses that border it are highly desirable as you effectively have an extension of a massive yard and great views relative to the suburban area surrounding it.

Somehow things changed and it's a bit surreal.

sentences.en: We built data centers for decades without issue.One I used to service frequently is actually surrounded by a large area of green-space, trees, marsh area, wildlife.
sentences.zh: 我们建立了数十年的数据中心，没有出现任何问题。我曾经经常服务的一个数据中心实际上周围有大片绿地、树木、沼泽区和野生动物。

sentences.en: The houses that border it are highly desirable as you effectively have an extension of a massive yard and great views relative to the suburban area surrounding it.Somehow things changed and it's a bit surreal.
sentences.zh: 与它接壤的房屋非常令人向往，因为您实际上拥有一个巨大庭院的延伸，并且相对于周围的郊区可以看到美丽的景色。不知何故，事情发生了变化，有点超现实。

blocks.html_en: The power density has been steadily increasing over the years, and then going from CPU to GPU makes it even bigger. This wouldn't be such a big issue if they actually took the time to build the infrastructure. But nobody has time, so it is gas turbines and evaporative cooling, and that makes a horrible neighbour.

blocks.html_en: First thing missing from this "article" is a number indicating how many people don't want AI datacentres anywhere. You can't claim that someone is a NIMBY if their stance is that the damned thing shouldn't be built anywhere.

sentences.en: First thing missing from this "article" is a number indicating how many people don't want AI datacentres anywhere.
sentences.zh: 这篇“文章”中首先缺少的是一个数字，表明有多少人不希望在任何地方建立人工智能数据中心。

Most Americans say "not in my backyard" to everything

I'm not saying they should want a data center in their backyard but headline wise "not in my backyard" is true of almost everything.

blocks.html_en:
I don't want a data center in my back yard because I do want housing in my back yard.

Once a data center shows up, that plot of land is locked out of more productive development for decades.

blocks.html_en: The whole world is California, and the most important thing we can do is pack more and more people into the same space. People from here, people from there. We want more crowdedness, and everyhing that comes with it: crime, noise, pollution, lack of social cohesion. Everything. Every place must be like this, and everything must grow forever.

sentences.en: The whole world is California, and the most important thing we can do is pack more and more people into the same space.
sentences.zh: 整个世界就是加州，我们能做的最重要的事情就是把越来越多的人塞进同一个空间。

sentences.en: People from here, people from there.
sentences.zh: 来自这里的人，来自那里的人。

sentences.zh: 我们想要更多的拥挤，以及随之而来的一切：犯罪、噪音、污染、缺乏社会凝聚力。

sentences.en: Everything.
sentences.zh: 一切。

sentences.en: Every place must be like this, and everything must grow forever.
sentences.zh: 每个地方都必须如此，一切都必须永远生长。

blocks.html_en: Negatives acknowledged, there are huge efficiency upsides to dense human living. Everything becomes smoother, faster, less wasteful. You get a society that leverages economies of scale for everything.

sentences.zh: 你会得到一个利用规模经济来解决一切问题的社会。

blocks.html_en: In some areas, density is appropriate, and in some areas it's not. I wish we could talk about housing density and "build more housing" as if there was not one universal solution that worked everywhere and for everyone. Dense urban living is not appropriate in a rural or suburban town. Single family homes are not appropriate in a high-demand urban area. Different people want different things, too. No matter how efficient and good for the environment it is, I don't want to live in a concrete honeycomb in the middle of a loud city.

sentences.en: In some areas, density is appropriate, and in some areas it's not.
sentences.zh: 在某些地区，密度是合适的，而在某些地区则是不合适的。

sentences.en: I wish we could talk about housing density and "build more housing" as if there was not one universal solution that worked everywhere and for everyone.
sentences.zh: 我希望我们能够谈论住房密度并“建造更多住房”，就好像没有一种通用解决方案可以在任何地方和每个人身上发挥作用一样。

sentences.en: Dense urban living is not appropriate in a rural or suburban town.
sentences.zh: 密集的城市生活不适合农村或郊区城镇。

sentences.en: Single family homes are not appropriate in a high-demand urban area.
sentences.zh: 单户住宅不适合高需求的城市地区。

sentences.en: Different people want different things, too.
sentences.zh: 不同的人想要的东西也不同。

sentences.en: No matter how efficient and good for the environment it is, I don't want to live in a concrete honeycomb in the middle of a loud city.
sentences.zh: 无论它多么高效、多么环保，我都不想住在喧闹城市中心的混凝土蜂窝里。

blocks.html_en:
> I don't want to live in a concrete honeycomb in the middle of a loud city.

Yes, this is the crux. Loud, smelly, concrete cities suck. And you’re right that density alone would lead to this.

What we’d ideally want is cities that can be dense without being loud, smelly, and dangerous. These cities exist: Amsterdam, Copenhagen, Zurich, Utrecht, Freiburg, etc.

We can’t do this in the US because we can’t remove the source of the noise/danger/stink/concrete problem: cars.

And that’s the catch 22: In the US everyone gets around by car, so density becomes undesirable. We can’t densify because density + cars is bad, and we can’t reduce cars because it’s not dense enough for public transit to get better.

Individuals get stuck in a prisoner’s dilemma where driving is safer, more comfortable, and faster than transit. So every individual with the means to drive chooses to drive. Public transit becomes branded as something for poor people. The concrete honeycomb fills up. The downtown becomes a noisy disaster. People can no longer imagine that good cities are possible.

sentences.en: > I don't want to live in a concrete honeycomb in the middle of a loud city.Yes, this is the crux.
sentences.zh: > 我不想住在喧闹城市中心的混凝土蜂窝中。是的，这就是症结所在。

sentences.en: Loud, smelly, concrete cities suck.
sentences.zh: 吵闹、臭气熏天、混凝土城市很糟糕。

sentences.en: And you’re right that density alone would lead to this.What we’d ideally want is cities that can be dense without being loud, smelly, and dangerous.
sentences.zh: 你是对的，仅靠密度就会导致这种情况。我们理想的情况是，城市既可以密集，又不会喧闹、臭气和危险。

sentences.en: These cities exist: Amsterdam, Copenhagen, Zurich, Utrecht, Freiburg, etc.We can’t do this in the US because we can’t remove the source of the noise/danger/stink/concrete problem: cars.And that’s the catch 22: In the US everyone gets around by car, so density becomes undesirable.
sentences.zh: 这些城市存在：阿姆斯特丹、哥本哈根、苏黎世、乌得勒支、弗莱堡等。我们在美国做不到这一点，因为我们无法消除噪音/危险/臭味/混凝土问题的根源：汽车。这就是问题 22：在美国，每个人都开车出行，因此密度变得不可取。

sentences.en: We can’t densify because density + cars is bad, and we can’t reduce cars because it’s not dense enough for public transit to get better.Individuals get stuck in a prisoner’s dilemma where driving is safer, more comfortable, and faster than transit.
sentences.zh: 我们不能增加密度，因为密度+汽车不好，我们也不能减少汽车，因为它的密度不足以让公共交通变得更好。个人陷入囚徒困境，驾驶比交通更安全、更舒适、更快。

sentences.en: So every individual with the means to drive chooses to drive.
sentences.zh: 因此，每个有能力开车的人都会选择开车。

sentences.en: Public transit becomes branded as something for poor people.
sentences.zh: 公共交通被打上了为穷人服务的标签。

sentences.en: The concrete honeycomb fills up.
sentences.zh: 混凝土蜂窝填满。

sentences.en: The downtown becomes a noisy disaster.
sentences.zh: 市中心变成了一场喧闹的灾难。

sentences.en: People can no longer imagine that good cities are possible.
sentences.zh: 人们不再想象美好的城市是可能的。

blocks.html_en: What we’d ideally want is cities that can be dense without being loud, smelly, and dangerous. These cities exist: Amsterdam, Copenhagen, Zurich, Utrecht, Freiburg, etc.

sentences.en: What we’d ideally want is cities that can be dense without being loud, smelly, and dangerous.
sentences.zh: 我们理想中想要的城市既可以密集，又不会喧闹、臭气和危险。

sentences.en: These cities exist: Amsterdam, Copenhagen, Zurich, Utrecht, Freiburg, etc.
sentences.zh: 这些城市存在：阿姆斯特丹、哥本哈根、苏黎世、乌得勒支、弗莱堡等。

blocks.html_en: We can’t do this in the US because we can’t remove the source of the noise/danger/stink/concrete problem: cars.

sentences.en: We can’t do this in the US because we can’t remove the source of the noise/danger/stink/concrete problem: cars.
sentences.zh: 我们在美国做不到这一点，因为我们无法消除噪音/危险/臭味/混凝土问题的根源：汽车。

blocks.html_en: And that’s the catch 22: In the US everyone gets around by car, so density becomes undesirable. We can’t densify because density + cars is bad, and we can’t reduce cars because it’s not dense enough for public transit to get better.

sentences.en: And that’s the catch 22: In the US everyone gets around by car, so density becomes undesirable.
sentences.zh: 这就是陷阱 22：在美国，每个人都开车出行，因此密度变得不可取。

sentences.en: We can’t densify because density + cars is bad, and we can’t reduce cars because it’s not dense enough for public transit to get better.
sentences.zh: 我们不能增加密度，因为密度+汽车不好，我们也不能减少汽车，因为它的密度不足以让公共交通变得更好。

blocks.html_en: Individuals get stuck in a prisoner’s dilemma where driving is safer, more comfortable, and faster than transit. So every individual with the means to drive chooses to drive. Public transit becomes branded as something for poor people. The concrete honeycomb fills up. The downtown becomes a noisy disaster. People can no longer imagine that good cities are possible.

sentences.en: Individuals get stuck in a prisoner’s dilemma where driving is safer, more comfortable, and faster than transit.
sentences.zh: 人们陷入了囚徒困境，即驾驶比公共交通更安全、更舒适、更快捷。

sentences.en: So every individual with the means to drive chooses to drive.
sentences.zh: 因此，每个有能力开车的人都会选择开车。

sentences.en: Public transit becomes branded as something for poor people.
sentences.zh: 公共交通被打上了为穷人服务的标签。

sentences.en: The concrete honeycomb fills up.
sentences.zh: 混凝土蜂窝填满。

sentences.en: The downtown becomes a noisy disaster.
sentences.zh: 市中心变成了一场喧闹的灾难。

sentences.en: People can no longer imagine that good cities are possible.
sentences.zh: 人们不再想象美好的城市是可能的。

blocks.html_en:
I live in a city and the problems are:

1) sometimes it is noisy at night, usually because of people in cars, but sometimes because of people not in cars;

2) sometimes it takes longer than needed to walk somewhere, due to intersections

3) my house is not as big as I would like. (I think this applies to many people regardless of how big their house is.)

4) the busses are always late in winter

sentences.en: I live in a city and the problems are:1) sometimes it is noisy at night, usually because of people in cars, but sometimes because of people not in cars;2) sometimes it takes longer than needed to walk somewhere, due to intersections3) my house is not as big as I would like.
sentences.zh: 我住在城市里，问题是：1）有时晚上很吵，通常是因为车里的人，但有时是因为车里的人；2）有时由于十字路口，步行到某个地方需要的时间比需要的时间长3）我的房子没有我想要的那么大。

sentences.en: (I think this applies to many people regardless of how big their house is.)4) the busses are always late in winter
sentences.zh: （我认为这适用于很多人，无论他们的房子有多大。）4）冬天公共汽车总是晚点

blocks.html_en: I know I kicked off this discussion, but I have also bemoaned many times about the anti-car people being quasi religious in their anti-worship of cars. They talk about it like missionaries talk about jesus.

blocks.html_en:
Well that's no different than vegans. We "need them" to preach obnoxiously because cars are presumed indispensable.

Vegans gave us the Impossible Burger, which I'll eat instead of regular burgers when available. What might anti-car people trigger as a positive?

sentences.en: Well that's no different than vegans.
sentences.zh: 嗯，这和素食主义者没有什么不同。

sentences.en: We "need them" to preach obnoxiously because cars are presumed indispensable.Vegans gave us the Impossible Burger, which I'll eat instead of regular burgers when available.
sentences.zh: 我们“需要他们”进行令人讨厌的宣讲，因为汽车被认为是不可或缺的。素食主义者给了我们不可能的汉堡，如果有的话我会吃它而不是普通汉堡。

sentences.en: What might anti-car people trigger as a positive?
sentences.zh: 反汽车人士可能会引发什么积极的影响？

blocks.html_en:
The only difference between a rural or suburban town and the sprawl of a city is time, really. Lots of modern urban areas used to be suburban or essentially rural.

Anyway if you densify cities you can slow down the sprawl and maybe keep suburbs actually smaller? a few more ten story apartment blocks downtown might house the people who have to live in your area and commute.

sentences.en: The only difference between a rural or suburban town and the sprawl of a city is time, really.
sentences.zh: 事实上，农村或郊区城镇与城市扩张之间的唯一区别是时间。

sentences.en: Lots of modern urban areas used to be suburban or essentially rural.Anyway if you densify cities you can slow down the sprawl and maybe keep suburbs actually smaller?
sentences.zh: 许多现代城市地区曾经是郊区或本质上是农村。无论如何，如果你提高城市密度，你可以减缓城市的扩张，也许还能让郊区变得更小？

sentences.en: a few more ten story apartment blocks downtown might house the people who have to live in your area and commute.
sentences.zh: 市中心的十层公寓楼可能会容纳那些必须居住在您所在地区并通勤的人。

blocks.html_en:
>In some areas, density is appropriate, and in some areas it's not.

Indeed, the way we have effectively banned density everywhere is wildly inappropriate. Cities should allow dense development. That is, after all, the whole idea of cities: attain the benefits of agglomeration. Cities rejecting anything other than detached single family houses in most of their footprints is contradictory to their purpose.

sentences.en: >In some areas, density is appropriate, and in some areas it's not.Indeed, the way we have effectively banned density everywhere is wildly inappropriate.
sentences.zh: >在某些地区，密度是适当的，而在某些地区则不然。事实上，我们在各地有效禁止密度的方式是非常不合适的。

sentences.en: Cities should allow dense development.
sentences.zh: 城市应该允许密集发展。

sentences.en: That is, after all, the whole idea of cities: attain the benefits of agglomeration.
sentences.zh: 毕竟，这就是城市的整体理念：获得集聚的好处。

sentences.en: Cities rejecting anything other than detached single family houses in most of their footprints is contradictory to their purpose.
sentences.zh: 城市在其大部分足迹中拒绝除了独立的单户住宅以外的任何东西，这与它们的目的相矛盾。

blocks.html_en:
This is a theoretical ideal, where we ignore people's desire to own large tracts of land and live in quiet places. A mega-city where all the humans human, and then massive tracts of untouched wilderness shy of any industry that has to be there.

This is also why communist countries had stuff like dense housing in deeply rural areas, and why most people were shuffled into cities. Big houses and properties do nothing for the state and just encumber society. Way easier if everyone just lives in their tightly packed central shoe box.

sentences.en: This is a theoretical ideal, where we ignore people's desire to own large tracts of land and live in quiet places.
sentences.zh: 这是一个理论上的理想，我们忽略了人们想要拥有大片土地并居住在安静的地方的愿望。

sentences.en: A mega-city where all the humans human, and then massive tracts of untouched wilderness shy of any industry that has to be there.This is also why communist countries had stuff like dense housing in deeply rural areas, and why most people were shuffled into cities.
sentences.zh: 一个所有人类都居住的大城市，然后是大片未受破坏的荒野，缺乏任何工业。这也是为什么共产主义国家在农村地区拥有密集的住房之类的东西，以及为什么大多数人被转移到城市。

sentences.en: Big houses and properties do nothing for the state and just encumber society.
sentences.zh: 大房子和财产对国家没有任何帮助，只会阻碍社会。

sentences.en: Way easier if everyone just lives in their tightly packed central shoe box.
sentences.zh: 如果每个人都住在紧凑的中央鞋盒里，那就容易多了。

blocks.html_en:
America is so massive, these trillion dollar companies can surely build these out in the boonies with very few people around?

ETA: I would rather no data centers be built, but my point is if we have to, let’s minimize the number of people who are affected. Offer to move them if need be.

sentences.en: America is so massive, these trillion dollar companies can surely build these out in the boonies with very few people around?ETA: I would rather no data centers be built, but my point is if we have to, let’s minimize the number of people who are affected.
sentences.zh: 美国幅员辽阔，这些价值数万亿美元的公司肯定可以在周围人很少的郊区建立这些数据中心吗？ ETA：我宁愿不建立数据中心，但我的观点是，如果必须这样做，让我们尽量减少受影响的人数。

sentences.en: Offer to move them if need be.
sentences.zh: 如果需要，主动提出移动它们。

blocks.html_en:
It's generally more expensive for them when it comes to things like power, water and internet.

Boonies have very poor in each of those and building it up would take too long so throw another in Northern Virginia since they have expensive access to additional of that but at least it will become operational.

sentences.en: It's generally more expensive for them when it comes to things like power, water and internet.Boonies have very poor in each of those and building it up would take too long so throw another in Northern Virginia since they have expensive access to additional of that but at least it will become operational.
sentences.zh: 对于他们来说，电力、水和互联网等方面的费用通常更高。布尼区的每一项都非常差，建设起来需要很长时间，所以在北弗吉尼亚州再建一个，因为他们可以获得额外的费用，但至少它会投入使用。

blocks.html_en: And ruin the boonies too? American norms and laws are not conducive to making nice places to live, but they've skated by having enough land and car-first infrastructure. With the huge increase in warehouses and data centers in residential areas over the past 5-10 years, I wonder if there will be an inflection point where it finally needs to be dealt with.

sentences.en: And ruin the boonies too?
sentences.zh: 还毁掉了边界？

sentences.en: With the huge increase in warehouses and data centers in residential areas over the past 5-10 years, I wonder if there will be an inflection point where it finally needs to be dealt with.
sentences.zh: 随着过去5-10年住宅区仓库和数据中心的大量增加，我想知道是否会出现一个最终需要处理的拐点。

blocks.html_en:
We could in a vacuum, but they require human labor and human labor isn't found in the boonies.

It's the age old problem. The people in the cities cry that there are no jobs and the rural businesses cry that they can't find anyone to hire, with no great resolution as the businesses cannot afford to move into the city and the people don't want to travel to the boonies.

However, in this case, these data centers are so well capitalized that they can afford to move them into the cities right beside where the workers are located.

sentences.en: We could in a vacuum, but they require human labor and human labor isn't found in the boonies.It's the age old problem.
sentences.zh: 我们可以在真空中，但它们需要人力，而在郊区找不到人力。这是一个古老的问题。

sentences.en: The people in the cities cry that there are no jobs and the rural businesses cry that they can't find anyone to hire, with no great resolution as the businesses cannot afford to move into the city and the people don't want to travel to the boonies.However, in this case, these data centers are so well capitalized that they can afford to move them into the cities right beside where the workers are located.
sentences.zh: 城市里的人哭着没有工作，农村的企业哭着找不到人，没有很好的解决方案，因为企业负担不起搬进城市，人们也不想去郊区。然而，在这种情况下，这些数据中心资本充足，他们有能力把它们搬到工人所在的城市附近。

blocks.html_en:
They need to hire IT, maintainence, and security to run the datacenters, and there aren't many qualified people in the boonies. Small towns are better targets, and honestly also just objectively less damaging to the environment.

None of this NIMBYism is about the datacenter itself, it's all a protest against AI making the economy really shitty for them.

sentences.en: They need to hire IT, maintainence, and security to run the datacenters, and there aren't many qualified people in the boonies.
sentences.zh: 他们需要雇用 IT 人员、维护人员和安全人员来运行数据中心，而附近地区合格的人员并不多。

sentences.en: Small towns are better targets, and honestly also just objectively less damaging to the environment.None of this NIMBYism is about the datacenter itself, it's all a protest against AI making the economy really shitty for them.
sentences.zh: 小城镇是更好的目标，老实说，客观上对环境的破坏也较小。这种邻避主义与数据中心本身无关，这都是对人工智能的抗议，使经济对他们来说真的很糟糕。

blocks.html_en:
Sounds like they should build the equivalent of a little mining town out in the boonies too and start working on the housing crisis simultaneously. “Move to Nowhere, USA, and get cheap, new housing and a good-paying job” would get them the talent they want.

EDIT: yes, I know mining towns had their own share of problems (I’ve heard “16 tons” too), but knowing the hazards of the past, I think we can figure out a more equitable structure with this kind of setup.

sentences.en: Sounds like they should build the equivalent of a little mining town out in the boonies too and start working on the housing crisis simultaneously.
sentences.zh: 听起来他们也应该在边远地区建造一个相当于矿业小镇的地方，同时开始解决住房危机。

sentences.en: “Move to Nowhere, USA, and get cheap, new housing and a good-paying job” would get them the talent they want.EDIT: yes, I know mining towns had their own share of problems (I’ve heard “16 tons” too), but knowing the hazards of the past, I think we can figure out a more equitable structure with this kind of setup.
sentences.zh: “搬到美国的任何地方，获得便宜的新住房和高薪工作”将为他们提供他们想要的人才。编辑：是的，我知道采矿城镇也有自己的问题（我也听说过“16吨”），但了解过去的危险，我认为我们可以通过这种设置找出更公平的结构。

sentences.en: “Move to Nowhere, USA, and get cheap, new housing and a good-paying job” would get them the talent they want.
sentences.zh: “搬到美国的任何地方，获得便宜的新住房和高薪工作”将为他们带来他们想要的人才。

blocks.html_en: EDIT: yes, I know mining towns had their own share of problems (I’ve heard “16 tons” too), but knowing the hazards of the past, I think we can figure out a more equitable structure with this kind of setup.

sentences.en: EDIT: yes, I know mining towns had their own share of problems (I’ve heard “16 tons” too), but knowing the hazards of the past, I think we can figure out a more equitable structure with this kind of setup.
sentences.zh: 编辑：是的，我知道采矿城镇也有自己的问题（我也听说过“16吨”），但了解过去的危险，我认为我们可以通过这种设置找出更公平的结构。

blocks.html_en:
Mining towns used to work in the days when one parent worked full-time and one parent raised kids full-time, and job security, unions, and pensions were a thing.

In modern times, both parents often have career ambitions, and the modern capitalist economy and housing pricing has greedily recalibrated to every family needing two incomes. So you're not going to have many families willing to move to the boonies unless you (at the bare minimum) hand out doubly-high salaries.

Very likely that's not enough though, as it's also a massive financial risk to them if you can just lay them off after the next earnings report with no pension to continue supporting them. Most modern families would rather stay where jobs are slightly more likely to exist.

sentences.en: Mining towns used to work in the days when one parent worked full-time and one parent raised kids full-time, and job security, unions, and pensions were a thing.In modern times, both parents often have career ambitions, and the modern capitalist economy and housing pricing has greedily recalibrated to every family needing two incomes.
sentences.zh: 过去，矿业城镇的工作方式是，父母一方全职工作，一方全职抚养孩子，工作保障、工会和养老金都是一回事。在现代，父母双方往往都有职业抱负，现代资本主义经济和房价已经贪婪地重新调整，以适应每个需要两份收入的家庭。

sentences.en: So you're not going to have many families willing to move to the boonies unless you (at the bare minimum) hand out doubly-high salaries.Very likely that's not enough though, as it's also a massive financial risk to them if you can just lay them off after the next earnings report with no pension to continue supporting them.
sentences.zh: 因此，除非你（至少）提供双倍高的工资，否则不会有很多家庭愿意搬到郊区。但这很可能还不够，因为如果你可以在下一次收益报告后解雇他们，而没有养老金继续支持他们，这对他们来说也是一个巨大的财务风险。

sentences.en: Most modern families would rather stay where jobs are slightly more likely to exist.
sentences.zh: 大多数现代家庭宁愿留在更有可能存在工作的地方。

blocks.html_en:
> “Move to Nowhere, USA, and get cheap, new housing and a good-paying job”

These types of opportunities already exist but are rarely taken up upon. More of them means more jobs unfilled.

When those mining towns were the way of the world people moved around a lot more. It was no big deal to pack your bags and move there. While census data only started being collected from the 1950s onward, it is estimated that ~40% of the population would move in a given year at the hight of the mining booms. Even in the 1950s, when we started collecting concrete data, around 30% of the population would move in a given year. Now only around 8% of the population will move in a given year.

Nowadays there is an expectation that you can stay put. For example, if you watch housing affordability discussions closely you'll notice the theme is always "I cannot afford a house where I am currently located", not "I cannot afford a house anywhere in the world". Attitudes about living arrangements have changed as compared to the past and the AI companies are not about to change it back any time soon. They have to work with people they want to hire, and that means putting the work where the people are already found.

sentences.zh: 收拾行李搬到那里并没有什么大不了的。

sentences.en: Even in the 1950s, when we started collecting concrete data, around 30% of the population would move in a given year.
sentences.zh: 即使在 20 世纪 50 年代，当我们开始收集具体数据时，也有大约 30% 的人口会在某一年迁移。

blocks.html_en:
You need on the order of 15 permanent onsite employees to run a datacenter; 5 each for 24/7 coverage of security, building maintenance, and tech. You probably don't really need 24/7 onsite for building maintenance or tech. Traveling employees or (probably traveling) contractors can handle projects that exceed the local scope.

Should be able to locally source security and building maintenance, if the pay is good. These are skills that are needed everywhere. The onsite tech can likely be sourced locally too: first and foremost, they're remote hands ... knowledgeable remote hands are nicer, but the mandatory skills are: read and follow directions, notice and report unusual circumstances, lift and manipulate heavy objects. It's not rocket surgery.

If you put your datacenter in the near boonies, say 30 minutes away from the nearest suburb, you can usually still find the big lots you need, and people can commute from there: a 30 minute commute with no traffic isn't too awful. Of course, you'll have neighbors on large lots who didn't think through that living out on the boonies with no zoning meant noisy neighbors could move in. :P

sentences.en: You probably don't really need 24/7 onsite for building maintenance or tech.
sentences.zh: 您可能并不真正需要 24/7 现场进行建筑维护或技术。

sentences.en: Traveling employees or (probably traveling) contractors can handle projects that exceed the local scope.
sentences.zh: 出差员工或（可能是出差）承包商可以处理超出当地范围的项目。

blocks.html_en: Should be able to locally source security and building maintenance, if the pay is good. These are skills that are needed everywhere. The onsite tech can likely be sourced locally too: first and foremost, they're remote hands ... knowledgeable remote hands are nicer, but the mandatory skills are: read and follow directions, notice and report unusual circumstances, lift and manipulate heavy objects. It's not rocket surgery.

sentences.en: Should be able to locally source security and building maintenance, if the pay is good.
sentences.zh: 如果报酬不错，应该能够在当地提供安保和建筑维护服务。

sentences.en: These are skills that are needed everywhere.
sentences.zh: 这些是到处都需要的技能。

sentences.en: The onsite tech can likely be sourced locally too: first and foremost, they're remote hands ...
sentences.zh: 现场技术也可能来自本地：首先也是最重要的是，他们是远程操作......

sentences.en: knowledgeable remote hands are nicer, but the mandatory skills are: read and follow directions, notice and report unusual circumstances, lift and manipulate heavy objects.
sentences.zh: 知识渊博的遥控手会更好，但必备技能是：阅读和遵循指示、注意和报告异常情况、举起和操纵重物。

sentences.en: It's not rocket surgery.
sentences.zh: 这不是火箭手术。

blocks.html_en: If you put your datacenter in the near boonies, say 30 minutes away from the nearest suburb, you can usually still find the big lots you need, and people can commute from there: a 30 minute commute with no traffic isn't too awful. Of course, you'll have neighbors on large lots who didn't think through that living out on the boonies with no zoning meant noisy neighbors could move in. :P

sentences.en: If you put your datacenter in the near boonies, say 30 minutes away from the nearest suburb, you can usually still find the big lots you need, and people can commute from there: a 30 minute commute with no traffic isn't too awful.
sentences.zh: 如果您将数据中心放在附近的郊区，例如距离最近的郊区 30 分钟路程，您通常仍然可以找到所需的大地块，并且人们可以从那里通勤：30 分钟的通勤时间且没有交通堵塞并不算太糟糕。

sentences.en: Of course, you'll have neighbors on large lots who didn't think through that living out on the boonies with no zoning meant noisy neighbors could move in. :P
sentences.zh: 当然，你会有大片土地上的邻居，他们没有想到住在郊区，没有分区意味着吵闹的邻居可以搬进来。:P

blocks.html_en: I don't consider 30 minutes away from town the "boonies", that's more like a normal commute. The boonies would be like a 3 hour drive in the Sonora desert.

sentences.en: I don't consider 30 minutes away from town the "boonies", that's more like a normal commute.
sentences.zh: 我不认为距城镇 30 分钟的路程是“郊区”，这更像是正常的通勤。

sentences.en: The boonies would be like a 3 hour drive in the Sonora desert.
sentences.zh: 这些郊区就像在索诺拉沙漠开车 3 小时一样。

blocks.html_en:
Well, "boonies" is pretty ill defined, but my criteria is most homes are are 10+ acre lots, and large lots are widely available.

You want your datacenter to be in reach of interstate highways (rail might be nice, too), two electrical substations [1], and close to fiber that's already run. That restricts your available boonies, but there's still lots outside of the east coast.

[1] yes, you'll have onsite generation, and yes, you might plan to run from onsite generation most of the time, but you still want to have the option for utility power, and it's nice to have two substations to get more uptime, subject to more transfer switch complexity.

sentences.en: Well, "boonies" is pretty ill defined, but my criteria is most homes are are 10+ acre lots, and large lots are widely available.You want your datacenter to be in reach of interstate highways (rail might be nice, too), two electrical substations [1], and close to fiber that's already run.
sentences.zh: 好吧，“郊区”的定义相当模糊，但我的标准是大多数房屋的面积都在 10 英亩以上，而且大面积的土地广泛可用。您希望您的数据中心位于州际高速公路（铁路也可能不错）、两个变电站 [1] 的范围内，并且靠近已运行的光纤。

sentences.en: That restricts your available boonies, but there's still lots outside of the east coast.[1] yes, you'll have onsite generation, and yes, you might plan to run from onsite generation most of the time, but you still want to have the option for utility power, and it's nice to have two substations to get more uptime, subject to more transfer switch complexity.
sentences.zh: 这限制了您可用的奖励，但在东海岸以外仍然有很多奖励。[1]是的，您将拥有现场发电，是的，您可能计划在大多数时间从现场发电运行，但您仍然希望选择公用电源，并且最好有两个变电站以获得更多正常运行时间，但会受到更多转换开关复杂性的影响。

blocks.html_en:
While most people wouldn't hesitate to travel 30 minutes out of town, the generally don't ever think to see if jobs exist 30 minutes out of town, and thus won't find the work there. When looking for work they search within the city they live in, not the next town over with a name they might not even know. This is a real problem for a lot of businesses that exist 30 minutes out of town.

In fairness, the companies behind these data centres are well known and have endless marketing budgets, so they can likely find a way to close that gap where the typical rural-based business cannot, but at the same time why take the risk when you can just as easily build the datacenter right beside the person you want to hire?

sentences.en: While most people wouldn't hesitate to travel 30 minutes out of town, the generally don't ever think to see if jobs exist 30 minutes out of town, and thus won't find the work there.
sentences.zh: 虽然大多数人会毫不犹豫地前往出城 30 分钟的地方，但他们通常不会想到去出城 30 分钟的地方是否有工作机会，因此不会在那里找到工作。

sentences.en: When looking for work they search within the city they live in, not the next town over with a name they might not even know.
sentences.zh: 当寻找工作时，他们会在自己居住的城市内进行搜索，而不是下一个他们甚至可能不知道名字的城镇。

sentences.en: This is a real problem for a lot of businesses that exist 30 minutes out of town.In fairness, the companies behind these data centres are well known and have endless marketing budgets, so they can likely find a way to close that gap where the typical rural-based business cannot, but at the same time why take the risk when you can just as easily build the datacenter right beside the person you want to hire?
sentences.zh: 对于距离城镇 30 分钟路程的许多企业来说，这是一个真正的问题。公平地说，这些数据中心背后的公司众所周知，并且拥有无尽的营销预算，因此他们很可能找到一种方法来缩小典型农村企业无法做到的差距，但同时，当您可以轻松地将数据中心建立在您想要雇用的人员旁边时，为什么还要冒险呢？

> so they can likely find a way to close that gap

I mean, typically you just list your job in the nearby major city, even when the site address isn't there. Same thing that you do when listing your hotel or your car for sale.

blocks.html_en:
30 minutes out of town isn't nearby anymore. The highways are surprisingly efficient. And consider that it is more like a 1-1.5 hour drive for someone living on the opposing end of the city — possibly more if it is a large city. Advertising a job 1.5 hours away as if it is local doesn't tend to win you any admiration from job seekers. Maybe AI companies are prominent enough that they can piss off workers and still hire them in the end, but that doesn't fly for most businesses.

But, again, why take the risk when you can just as easily put the work right beside the workers?

sentences.en: 30 minutes out of town isn't nearby anymore.
sentences.zh: 距离市区 30 分钟路程已经不复存在了。

sentences.en: The highways are surprisingly efficient.
sentences.zh: 高速公路的效率出奇地高。

sentences.en: And consider that it is more like a 1-1.5 hour drive for someone living on the opposing end of the city — possibly more if it is a large city.
sentences.zh: 考虑一下，对于住在城市另一端的人来说，开车需要 1-1.5 小时——如果是大城市，可能需要更多时间。

sentences.en: Advertising a job 1.5 hours away as if it is local doesn't tend to win you any admiration from job seekers.
sentences.zh: 在距离 1.5 小时路程的地方做广告，就好像它是本地的一样，并不会给你赢得求职者的任何钦佩。

sentences.en: Maybe AI companies are prominent enough that they can piss off workers and still hire them in the end, but that doesn't fly for most businesses.But, again, why take the risk when you can just as easily put the work right beside the workers?
sentences.zh: 也许人工智能公司足够突出，他们可以激怒工人，但最终仍然雇用他们，但这对大多数企业来说并不可行。但是，当你可以轻松地将工作放在工人旁边时，为什么还要冒险呢？

blocks.html_en: The datacenter outrage is mislabeled. It's actually zoning outrage, caused by local governments that aren't taking their citizen's concerns seriously. It just so happens that datacenters are the hottest (no pun intended) industrial construction option right now.

sentences.en: The datacenter outrage is mislabeled.

I don’t think any of these examples represent _successful_ opposition, though. Except maybe the warming center for homeless people? I can’t tell if it got built or not.

These examples basically amount to “some people didn’t like this”, which doesn’t mean that the balance of public opinion wasn’t favourable. Unanimous support is too high a bar.

blocks.html_en:
I find what people want from the data center build-out is pretty reasonable:

1. Don't build loud machines across the street from someone's house. 2. Don't drive up water/electricity bills by pulling from the municipal grid. 3. Don't burn the earth with CO2. 4. Don't facilitate techno-fascist surveillance. 5. Pay a lot of taxes.

But, when I see people complain about data centers, I usually see a spiral of rage-bait based on mis-framing, conjecture and repeating stuff others simply made up to get everyone mad. The online discussion tone is reaching a shrieking pink-froth level.

What I'm finding in my lazy research on the subject is that recent and planned datacenters

1. Are exceedingly rarely built where anyone can hear them. But, occasionally are. Thus ragebait articles. 2. Pull from muni grids for small scale centers. But, the hyperscalers are independent. 3. Hyperscalers are burning way too much natural gas. That's bad. But, not using nearly as much water as people think. That's good. 4. Techno-fascist surveillance doesn't need 1000s of datacenters. Just a handful off in the corner is plenty. 5. Datacenters usually are paying a lot of taxes. I think this drives the majority of the push from politicians. And, there's a minority from corruption.

Frankly, the biggest issue folks should be pushing for is to require hyperscalers build out solar power instead of natural gas. That will be a hard fight because it requires more up-front time and investment. From there, ensuring small scale centers don't affect people's bills is a good fight. But, passing laws to ban datacenters seems performative compared to passing laws to regulate their power and water impact.

sentences.en: I find what people want from the data center build-out is pretty reasonable:1.
sentences.zh: 我发现人们对数据中心扩建的期望是相当合理的：1。

sentences.en: Don't build loud machines across the street from someone's house.
sentences.zh: 不要在某人家的街对面建造噪音很大的机器。

sentences.en: Don't drive up water/electricity bills by pulling from the municipal grid.
sentences.zh: 不要通过从市政电网拉电来增加水费/电费。

sentences.en: Don't burn the earth with CO2.
sentences.zh: 不要用二氧化碳燃烧地球。

sentences.en: Don't facilitate techno-fascist surveillance.
sentences.zh: 不要促进技术法西斯监视。

sentences.en: Pay a lot of taxes.But, when I see people complain about data centers, I usually see a spiral of rage-bait based on mis-framing, conjecture and repeating stuff others simply made up to get everyone mad.
sentences.zh: 缴纳大量税款。但是，当我看到人们抱怨数据中心时，我通常会看到基于错误框架、猜测和重复其他人为了让每个人都生气而编造的东西的愤怒诱饵的螺旋式上升。

sentences.en: The online discussion tone is reaching a shrieking pink-froth level.What I'm finding in my lazy research on the subject is that recent and planned datacenters1.
sentences.zh: 在线讨论的基调已经达到了令人尖叫的粉红色泡沫水平。我在对这个主题的懒惰研究中发现的是最近和计划中的数据中心1。

sentences.en: Are exceedingly rarely built where anyone can hear them.
sentences.zh: 很少建在任何人都能听到的地方。

sentences.en: But, occasionally are.
sentences.zh: 但是，偶尔是。

sentences.en: Thus ragebait articles.
sentences.zh: 因此，愤怒诱饵文章。

sentences.en: Pull from muni grids for small scale centers.
sentences.zh: 从小型中心的市政电网中拉取。

sentences.en: But, the hyperscalers are independent.
sentences.zh: 但是，超大规模企业是独立的。

sentences.en: Hyperscalers are burning way too much natural gas.
sentences.zh: 超大规模企业燃烧了太多的天然气。

sentences.en: That's bad.
sentences.zh: 那很糟糕。

sentences.en: But, not using nearly as much water as people think.
sentences.zh: 但是，使用的水量并不像人们想象的那么多。

sentences.en: That's good.
sentences.zh: 那挺好的。

sentences.en: Techno-fascist surveillance doesn't need 1000s of datacenters.
sentences.zh: 技术法西斯监视不需要数千个数据中心。

sentences.en: Just a handful off in the corner is plenty.
sentences.zh: 只要在角落里放一把就足够了。

sentences.en: Datacenters usually are paying a lot of taxes.
sentences.zh: 数据中心通常要缴纳大量税款。

sentences.en: I think this drives the majority of the push from politicians.
sentences.zh: 我认为这推动了政治家的大部分推动。

sentences.en: And, there's a minority from corruption.Frankly, the biggest issue folks should be pushing for is to require hyperscalers build out solar power instead of natural gas.
sentences.zh: 而且，有少数人来自腐败。坦率地说，人们应该推动的最大问题是要求超大规模企业建设太阳能而不是天然气。

sentences.en: That will be a hard fight because it requires more up-front time and investment.
sentences.zh: 这将是一场艰苦的战斗，因为它需要更多的前期时间和投资。

sentences.en: From there, ensuring small scale centers don't affect people's bills is a good fight.
sentences.zh: 从这里开始，确保小型中心不会影响人们的账单是一场很好的斗争。

sentences.en: But, passing laws to ban datacenters seems performative compared to passing laws to regulate their power and water impact.
sentences.zh: 但是，与通过法律来规范数据中心的电力和水影响相比，通过禁止数据中心的法律似乎更具执行力。

sentences.en: But, the hyperscalers are independent.
sentences.zh: 但是，超大规模企业是独立的。

sentences.en: Hyperscalers are burning way too much natural gas.
sentences.zh: 超大规模企业燃烧了太多的天然气。

sentences.en: That's bad.
sentences.zh: 那很糟糕。

sentences.en: But, not using nearly as much water as people think.
sentences.zh: 但是，使用的水量并不像人们想象的那么多。

sentences.en: That's good.
sentences.zh: 那挺好的。

sentences.en: Techno-fascist surveillance doesn't need 1000s of datacenters.
sentences.zh: 技术法西斯监视不需要数千个数据中心。

sentences.en: Just a handful off in the corner is plenty.
sentences.zh: 只要在角落里放一把就足够了。

sentences.en: Datacenters usually are paying a lot of taxes.
sentences.zh: 数据中心通常要缴纳大量税款。

sentences.en: I think this drives the majority of the push from politicians.
sentences.zh: 我认为这推动了政治家的大部分推动。

sentences.en: And, there's a minority from corruption.
sentences.zh: 而且，还有少数人腐败。

blocks.html_en: Frankly, the biggest issue folks should be pushing for is to require hyperscalers build out solar power instead of natural gas. That will be a hard fight because it requires more up-front time and investment. From there, ensuring small scale centers don't affect people's bills is a good fight. But, passing laws to ban datacenters seems performative compared to passing laws to regulate their power and water impact.

sentences.en: Frankly, the biggest issue folks should be pushing for is to require hyperscalers build out solar power instead of natural gas.
sentences.zh: 坦率地说，人们应该推动的最大问题是要求超大规模企业建设太阳能而不是天然气。

sentences.en: That will be a hard fight because it requires more up-front time and investment.
sentences.zh: 这将是一场艰苦的战斗，因为它需要更多的前期时间和投资。

sentences.en: From there, ensuring small scale centers don't affect people's bills is a good fight.
sentences.zh: 从这里开始，确保小型中心不会影响人们的账单是一场很好的斗争。

sentences.en: But, passing laws to ban datacenters seems performative compared to passing laws to regulate their power and water impact.
sentences.zh: 但是，与通过法律来规范数据中心的电力和水影响相比，通过禁止数据中心的法律似乎更具执行力。

blocks.html_en:
I did some analysis on this, although I’m not a finance expert. If you put $100M of GPUs in a Virginia datacenter then you might pay $2M per year in local taxes over the 5 year life of the equipment. (I don’t think tech companies tend to pay much corporate income tax.) If you took the same money and put up an office with 150 people making $150k/year, you’d pay less property tax but like $7M in income taxes, not to mention the other taxes those people pay as they go about their lives nearby, and the fact that their incomes are re-invested in the community. So companies shifting their spending from employees to GPUs seems to massively reduce their tax burden, and datacenters are a vastly worse deal than offices for whatever community ends up stuck with them.

It does seem like civil society could hold out for a better deal here, and not lose what little tax base we are currently getting from these tech giants.

sentences.en: I did some analysis on this, although I’m not a finance expert.
sentences.zh: 虽然我不是金融专家，但我对此做了一些分析。

sentences.en: If you put $100M of GPUs in a Virginia datacenter then you might pay $2M per year in local taxes over the 5 year life of the equipment.
sentences.zh: 如果您将 1 亿美元的 GPU 放入弗吉尼亚数据中心，那么在设备的 5 年使用寿命内，您每年可能需要缴纳 200 万美元的地方税。

sentences.en: (I don’t think tech companies tend to pay much corporate income tax.)
sentences.zh: （我认为科技公司不会缴纳太多企业所得税。）

sentences.en: If you took the same money and put up an office with 150 people making $150k/year, you’d pay less property tax but like $7M in income taxes, not to mention the other taxes those people pay as they go about their lives nearby, and the fact that their incomes are re-invested in the community.
sentences.zh: 如果你用同样的钱建立一个有 150 人的办公室，每年赚取 15 万美元，你会少付财产税，但要缴纳 700 万美元的所得税，更不用说人们在附近生活时缴纳的其他税款，以及他们的收入重新投资于社区的事实。

sentences.en: So companies shifting their spending from employees to GPUs seems to massively reduce their tax burden, and datacenters are a vastly worse deal than offices for whatever community ends up stuck with them.It does seem like civil society could hold out for a better deal here, and not lose what little tax base we are currently getting from these tech giants.
sentences.zh: 因此，公司将支出从员工转移到 GPU 似乎大大减轻了他们的税收负担，而对于最终陷入困境的社区来说，数据中心比办公室要糟糕得多。民间社会似乎确实可以在这里坚持更好的协议，而不会失去我们目前从这些科技巨头那里获得的一点点税基。

blocks.html_en:
Absolutely this. If you think about different types of objections to nearby data centers and do some pretty minimal research, you come up with a reasonable set of solutions that most people would agree on.

That's not what people are doing though. People are genuinely afraid for the future, and much of the data center backlash is driven by emotion. You can see this reflected in populist political movements. People have a (well-founded) sense that those in power don't have their interests in mind and they're lashing out without much thought.

sentences.en: Absolutely this.
sentences.zh: 绝对是这个。

sentences.en: If you think about different types of objections to nearby data centers and do some pretty minimal research, you come up with a reasonable set of solutions that most people would agree on.That's not what people are doing though.
sentences.zh: 如果您考虑对附近数据中心的不同类型的反对意见并进行一些相当简单的研究，您就会想出一套大多数人都会同意的合理解决方案。但这并不是人们正在做的事情。

sentences.en: People are genuinely afraid for the future, and much of the data center backlash is driven by emotion.
sentences.zh: 人们确实对未来感到恐惧，而对数据中心的强烈反对很大程度上是由情绪驱动的。

sentences.en: You can see this reflected in populist political movements.
sentences.zh: 你可以在民粹主义政治运动中看到这一点。

sentences.en: People have a (well-founded) sense that those in power don't have their interests in mind and they're lashing out without much thought.
sentences.zh: 人们有一种（有充分根据的）感觉，即当权者不考虑他们的利益，他们不假思索地猛烈抨击。

blocks.html_en:
Simple, make it legally required that all new datacenters being built by <LLM_COMPANY> is built in the literal backyard of the primary residence of the children of the Csuites of said <LLM_COMPANY>.

Everyone should be in support of this idea.

Everyone else gets to be free from datacenter pollution, and the Csuites get their datacenters.

Win win.

blocks.html_en:
I would flip my bitch switch if a datacenter was stood up near me, where I could hear it. I am on 5 acres in rural Michigan surrounded by peace and quiet. I specifically came here for this reason. At the same time, I understand that we need datacenters. And tbh, Michigan is under-served in this space (ex, most of my queries thru the Cloudflare network have to go to Chicago first), and I wish we had more. But not close to residential areas. Not on top of valuable farmland.

My wacky idea was that datacenters should be built on EPA superfund sites. It becomes a win-win. Datacenter gets land that humans cannot occupy, helps/funds partial cleanup, slaps a big ass concrete building down on top of the rest of the mess and kills two birds with one stone. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Superfund_sites

Also thoughts of mine recently: why are we not doing subterranean datacenters, why are we not doing tall/skinny skyscraper like datacenters (versus enormous sprawling warehouse-style).

blocks.html_en:
> Also thoughts of mine recently: why are we not doing subterranean datacenters, why are we not doing tall/skinny skyscraper like datacenters (versus enormous sprawling warehouse-style).

Because it costs an order of magnitude more money to build underground or build a skyscraper than it does it build a precast concrete tilt-up warehouse.

You can build a single story warehouse building with as large of a floor plate as you want, it could be hundreds of square miles if you didn’t need any utilities inside of it. Obviously you do need utilities which limit the overall building size because of things like voltage drop, pump sizing, etc.

Excavating that much earth, building a concrete underground structure, and then covering it, or erecting a steel and concrete tower is much more work than having precast wall sections shipped in from the prefab plant which are then tilted into place with cranes and secured to the concrete slab foundation.

sentences.en: > Also thoughts of mine recently: why are we not doing subterranean datacenters, why are we not doing tall/skinny skyscraper like datacenters (versus enormous sprawling warehouse-style).Because it costs an order of magnitude more money to build underground or build a skyscraper than it does it build a precast concrete tilt-up warehouse.You can build a single story warehouse building with as large of a floor plate as you want, it could be hundreds of square miles if you didn’t need any utilities inside of it.
sentences.zh: > 我最近的想法是：为什么我们不做地下数据中心，为什么我们不做像数据中心那样高/瘦的摩天大楼（而不是巨大的杂乱仓库式）。因为建造地下或建造摩天大楼比建造预制混凝土倾斜仓库要花费一个数量级的资金。你可以建造一座单层仓库建筑，其楼板面积有多大，如果你不需要的话，可能有数百平方英里其中的任何实用程序。

sentences.en: Obviously you do need utilities which limit the overall building size because of things like voltage drop, pump sizing, etc.Excavating that much earth, building a concrete underground structure, and then covering it, or erecting a steel and concrete tower is much more work than having precast wall sections shipped in from the prefab plant which are then tilted into place with cranes and secured to the concrete slab foundation.
sentences.zh: 显然，您确实需要公用设施，因为电压降、泵尺寸等因素限制了整体建筑尺寸。挖掘那么多泥土，建造混凝土地下结构，然后覆盖它，或者竖起一座钢和混凝土塔，比从预制工厂运来预制墙部分要多得多的工作，然后用起重机倾斜到位并固定到混凝土板基础上。

Excavating that much earth, building a concrete underground structure, and then covering it, or erecting a steel and concrete tower is much more work than having precast wall sections shipped in from the prefab plant which are then tilted into place with cranes and secured to the concrete slab foundation.

blocks.html_en: The other day I was talking with two friends—one programmer, one musician, both of fairly conservative political persuasion—and the topic of “AI data centers” came up. I said something to the effect of, “y'know, all that talk about how they just use all up all the water from wherever they're built is total propaganda nonsense, right?” and both were genuinely taken aback by this statement. As far as they were concerned, “AI data centers” were something a Captain Planet villain would build, just absolute ecological disasters, inherently. I'm relatively neutral when it comes to this stuff but man, the propaganda against “AI data centers” really works!

sentences.en: The other day I was talking with two friends—one programmer, one musician, both of fairly conservative political persuasion—and the topic of “AI data centers” came up.
sentences.zh: 有一天，我和两个朋友聊天——一个是程序员，一个是音乐家，两人的政治倾向都相当保守——谈到了“人工智能数据中心”的话题。

sentences.en: I said something to the effect of, “y'know, all that talk about how they just use all up all the water from wherever they're built is total propaganda nonsense, right?”
sentences.zh: 我说了一些大意的话，“你知道，所有那些谈论他们如何耗尽他们建造的地方的所有水的完全是宣传废话，对吧？”

sentences.en: and both were genuinely taken aback by this statement.
sentences.zh: 两人都对这句话感到非常惊讶。

sentences.en: As far as they were concerned, “AI data centers” were something a Captain Planet villain would build, just absolute ecological disasters, inherently.
sentences.zh: 在他们看来，“人工智能数据中心”是星球船长恶棍会建造的东西，本质上就是绝对的生态灾难。

sentences.en: I'm relatively neutral when it comes to this stuff but man, the propaganda against “AI data centers” really works!
sentences.zh: 当谈到这些东西时，我相对中立，但是伙计，针对“人工智能数据中心”的宣传确实有效！

blocks.html_en:
If every American skipped a single burger per year, that'd entirely offset the combined water use of all American datacenters.

Datacenters, in other words, do not consume all that much water in the grand scheme of things. There are many, many better targets if water conservation is a genuine concern.

sentences.en: If every American skipped a single burger per year, that'd entirely offset the combined water use of all American datacenters.Datacenters, in other words, do not consume all that much water in the grand scheme of things.
sentences.zh: 如果每个美国人每年不吃一个汉堡，那就完全抵消了所有美国数据中心的总用水量。换句话说，从总体上看，数据中心不会消耗那么多的水。

sentences.en: There are many, many better targets if water conservation is a genuine concern.
sentences.zh: 如果真正关心节约用水，还有很多很多更好的目标。

blocks.html_en:
Why am I supposed to take a janky-ass web visualization, completely devoid of citations as far as I can tell, and which was, itself, almost certainly vibecoded, at face value, as a valid argument?

I love how you went from posting links to articles to make arguments for you, to then editing your comment to link to a $250 book that I should buy and read, so it can make your argument for you.

sentences.en: Why am I supposed to take a janky-ass web visualization, completely devoid of citations as far as I can tell, and which was, itself, almost certainly vibecoded, at face value, as a valid argument?I love how you went from posting links to articles to make arguments for you, to then editing your comment to link to a $250 book that I should buy and read, so it can make your argument for you.
sentences.zh: 为什么我应该把一个糟糕的网络可视化作为一个有效的论点，据我所知，完全没有引文，而且它本身几乎肯定是经过振动编码的，从表面上看，这是一个有效的论点？我喜欢你从发布文章链接为你提出论点，然后编辑你的评论链接到一本我应该购买和阅读的 250 美元的书，这样它就可以为你提出论点。

blocks.html_en:
IMO NIMB should be illegal, so long as the builder meets standards. Maybe those standards should rise to include ambient noise requirements, heat dissipation, capital contributions to water/electricity supply, additional tax burden points (for land use without, maybe?).

But I also feel there needs to be an assertion that if you own the land it's your property and you're free to do most things with it, safely and politely. This is about property rights and freedoms.

sentences.en: IMO NIMB should be illegal, so long as the builder meets standards.
sentences.zh: IMO NIMB 应该是非法的，只要建筑商符合标准。

sentences.en: Maybe those standards should rise to include ambient noise requirements, heat dissipation, capital contributions to water/electricity supply, additional tax burden points (for land use without, maybe?).But I also feel there needs to be an assertion that if you own the land it's your property and you're free to do most things with it, safely and politely.
sentences.zh: 也许这些标准应该提高到包括环境噪音要求、散热、水/电供应的资本贡献、额外的税收负担点（对于土地使用，也许没有？）。但我也觉得需要有一个断言，如果你拥有土地，它就是你的财产，你可以自由地、安全和礼貌地用它做大多数事情。

sentences.en: This is about property rights and freedoms.
sentences.zh: 这是关于财产权和自由的。

blocks.html_en:
IMO NIMB should be illegal, so long as the builder meets standards. Maybe those standards should rise to include ambient noise requirements, heat dissipation, capital contributions to water/electricity supply, additional tax burden points (for land use without, maybe?).

But I also feel there needs to be an assertion that if you own the land it's your property and you're free to do most things with it, safely and politely. This is about property rights and freedoms.

blocks.html_en:
This is about property rights and freedoms.

Your property rights stop where my ears are. Or I have the freedom to not listen to your gas turbine. Or however one wishes to flip that on its head. Whether you own the property or not, that property isn’t in a vacuum.
